blocks:
Surah Yusuf

Meccan, and it is 111 verses.

Surah Yusuf (peace be upon him)

It is Meccan, and al-Mu'addal narrated from Ibn Abbas that four verses were revealed in Medina: three from its beginning, and the fourth is: لَقَدْ كانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ آياتٌ لِلسَّائِلِينَ ** (There is certainly a sign in [the story of] Joseph and his brothers for those who ask.) The number of its verses is one hundred and eleven verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الر ** (Alif, Lām, Rā.) Its meaning has preceded. تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ ** (These are the signs of the manifest Book.) Those verses are the verses of the Book whose matter is manifest in its miraculousness (الإعجاز), and whose meanings are clear to whoever reflects upon it.

(٢) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (Indeed, We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’an so that you may apply reason.) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا ** (in your language) لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** Meaning: so that you may understand it (تَفْقَهُوهُ) and encompass its meanings, for had We made it non-Arabic (أعجميّاً), it would have been confusing for you. In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Learn Arabic, for it is the speech of Allah with which He spoke to His creation."

(٣) نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ ** (3) We recount to you the best of narratives The best of recounting (أحسن الاقتصاص). Because He recounted it using the most marvelous styles (أبدع الأساليب), or (it is) the best of what is recounted, due to its inclusion of wonders (العجائب), wisdom (الحِكَم), and lessons (العِبَر). بِما أَوْحَيْنا ** by Our revelation (بإيحائنا) **إِلَيْكَ هذَا الْقُرْآنَ وَإِنْ كُنْتَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ لَمِنَ الْغافِلِينَ ** (3) by Our revealing to you this Quran, though you were among the oblivious before it. Concerning this story; it had not occurred to your mind, nor had it ever reached your hearing.

(٤) إِذْ قالَ يُوسُفُ لِأَبِيهِ (When Joseph said to his father,) Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Jacob was Isrāʾīl Allāh (إسرائيل الله), meaning the sincere one (خالص) of Allah, the son of Isaac, the Prophet of Allah (نبيّ اللهِ), the son of Abraham, the intimate friend of Allah (خليل الله). And in the Prophetic Hadith: The noble one (الكريم), son of the noble one, son of the noble one, son of the noble one: Joseph son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham. يا أَبَتِ (O my father!) Its origin is yā abī (يا أبي), and it was recited with the fathah on the tāʾ (التّاءِ) and with a pause on the hāʾ (الهاءِ). إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ (I saw) (This is) from a dream (الرّؤيا), not from sight (الرّؤية). أَحَدَ عَشَرَ كَوْكَباً وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِي ساجِدِينَ. (eleven planets, and the sun and the moon: I saw them prostrating before me.) In al-Khisal, narrated from Jabir ibn ʿAbd Allāh, he said: A man from the Jews, who was called Bishān the Jew, came to the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, inform me about the planets that Joseph saw prostrating. What are their names?" The Prophet of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم did not answer him regarding anything that day. He said: Then Gabriel descended and informed the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم of their names. He said: Then the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم sent for Bishān. When he came, the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Will you embrace Islam if I inform you of their names?" He said: "Yes." So the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: Ḥawbān (حوبان), al-Ṭāriq (الطارق), al-Dhubāl (الذّبال), Dhū al-Katfayn (ذو الكتفين), Qābis (قابس), Waththāb (وثاب), ʿAmūdān (عمودان), al-Faylaq (الفيلق), al-Muṣbiḥ (المصبح), al-Ṣadūḥ (الصّدوح), Dhū al-Furūgh (ذو الفروغ), al-Ḍiyāʾ (الضّياء), and al-Nūr (النّور). He saw them in the horizon of the sky prostrating to him. When Joseph related this to Jacob, Jacob said: "This is a scattered matter (أمر متشتّت) that Allah will gather later." Bishān then said: "By Allah, these are indeed their names!" Then he embraced Islam. Al-Qummi and al-ʿAyyāshī narrated from Jabir regarding the naming of the stars (النّجوم): They are al-Ṭāriq (الطارق) and Ḥawbān (حوبان), and he mentioned the like of it up to His saying: al-Ḍiyāʾ (الضّياء) and al-Nūr (النّور). He said: meaning the sun and the moon. He said: And all these planets (الكواكب) encompass the sky. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The interpretation (تأويل) of this dream is that he will rule Egypt, and his parents and brothers will enter upon him. As for the sun, it is Joseph's mother, Rachel (راحيل); and the moon is Jacob; and as for the eleven planets, they are his brothers. When they entered upon him, they prostrated in gratitude to Allah alone when they looked at him, and that prostration (السّجُود) was for Allah, the Most High. I say: And another narration will come: that the one who prostrated to him along with his father was his maternal aunt (خالته), not his mother.

(٥) قالَ يا بُنَيَ ** (5) He said, 'O my son! The word (بُنَيَ) is the diminutive form (تصغير) of (ابن) (son). He made it diminutive out of compassion (للشّفقة) and due to his young age (صغر السِّنّ). لا تَقْصُصْ رُؤْياكَ 'Do not recount your dream' The dream (الرّؤيا) is like sight (الرّؤية), except that it is specific to what occurs during sleep (في النّوم). عَلى إِخْوَتِكَ فَيَكِيدُوا لَكَ كَيْداً ** 'to your brothers, lest they devise a plot against you.' Meaning: lest they scheme (فيحتالوا) for your destruction (لاهلاكك) with a scheme (حيلة). The verb (يَكِيدُوا) implies the meaning of (يحتالوا) (to scheme), so it was made transitive using the particle (اللام) (لَكَ) to convey the meaning of both actions. إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ لِلْإِنْسانِ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ 'Indeed Satan is a manifest enemy to man.' Meaning: manifest in enmity (ظاهر العداوة). He feared for him the envy (حسد) of his brothers and their transgression (بغيهم) against him, because he knew the indication (دلالة) of his dream: that he would attain a great matter (أمراً عظيماً) concerning the honor of both abodes (شرف الدارين).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He had eleven brothers, and he had one brother from his mother named Benjamin (بنيامين). Joseph saw this dream when he was nine years old, and he recounted it to his father, who then said: **يا بُنَيَّ لا تَقْصُصْ ** (O my son! Do not recount...) the rest of the Ayah.

I say: What this narration indicates—that Joseph and Benjamin (بنيامين) shared the same mother—is the well-known (المشهور) and widely transmitted (المستفيض) view, narrated by al-Ayyashi and others. However, al-Ayyashi narrated another tradition: that he was the son of his maternal aunt (ابن خالته).

In some of what he narrates, the term "son of Yamil" (ابن ياميل) is applied to him, using the definite article (باللّام). In some of it, it is stated that Yamil (ياميل) is the name of Joseph's maternal aunt (خالة يوسف) and that she was the one who traveled with his father to Egypt. Most of these narrations will come in their proper places, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).

And sometimes, in some reports of al-Ayyashi, the name Ibn Yamin (ابن يامين) is found separated. The author of al-Qamus fixed the pronunciation as Binyamin (بنيامين) and said: "Do not say Ibn Yamin (ابن يامين)." As for the names of his other brothers, I have not found them fully enumerated in an infallible (معصوميّة) narration. It has been said that they are Judah (يهودا), Reuben (روبيل), Simeon (شمعون), Levi (لاوى), Zebulun (زبالون), and Issachar (يشجر). These six were from his maternal aunt's daughter, Leah (ليا), whom Jacob married first. Then he married her sister, Rachel (راحيل), who bore him Benjamin (بنيامين) and Joseph (يوسف). And four others—Dan (دان), Naphtali (نفتالي), Gad (حاد), and Asher (اشر)—were from two concubines (سريّتين), Zilpah (زلفة) and Bilhah (بلهة).

(6) وَكَذلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ ** He selects you رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحادِيثِ ** from the interpretation of the vision (الرّؤيا), because they are the narratives of the Angel (المَلَك) if they are truthful, and the narratives of the soul (النّفس) or Satan (الشيطان) if they are false. وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلى آلِ يَعْقُوبَ ** his family and his descendants, by joining the blessing of this world (الدّنيا) with the blessing of the Hereafter (الآخرة), by making them prophets (أنبياء) and kings (ملوكاً), then He transfers them to the bliss of the Hereafter (الآخرة) and the highest ranks (الدّرجات العلى) of Paradise (الجنّة). كَما أَتَمَّها عَلى أَبَوَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْحاقَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ ** of whom deserves the selection (الاجتباء) **حَكِيمٌ ** He does things as they ought to be done.

(6) Thus will your Lord choose you, and teach you the interpretation of dreams, and complete His blessing upon you and upon the progeny of Jacob, just as He completed it upon your fathers before, Abraham and Isaac. Indeed your Lord is all-knowing, all-wise."

(٧) لَقَدْ كانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ ** (7) Indeed there was in Joseph and his brothers i.e., in their story آياتٌ ** signs proofs of Allah's power and His wisdom, and signs of your prophethood. And it was recited (آية) (singular). لِلسَّائِلِينَ ** for those who ask** for whoever asked about their story.

(7) **لَقَدْ كانَ فِي يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِ آياتٌ لِلسَّائِلِينَ ** (7) There was certainly a sign in Joseph and his brothers for those who inquire.

In al-Jawami', it is narrated: That the Jews said to the chiefs of the polytheists: "Ask Muhammad why the family of Jacob (آل يعقوب) moved from Syria (الشام) to Egypt (مصر), and about the story of Joseph." He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) informed them of the story without hearing (it) or reading a book.

(٨) إِذْ قالُوا لَيُوسُفُ وَأَخُوهُ ٨ (8) When they said, 'Joseph and his brother (meaning) Benjamin (بنيامين) was specified with brotherhood because their mother was one. أَحَبُّ إِلى أَبِينا مِنَّا وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ ** are dearer to our father than we are, while we are a band. And the situation is that we are a strong group (جماعة أقوياء), more deserving of love than two small ones (صغيرين) in whom there is no sufficiency (كفاية). إِنَّ أَبانا لَفِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** Our father is indeed in manifest error.' Because of his preferring the less preferred (المفضول) and abandoning fairness (التعديل) in love.

(٩) اقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ أَوِ اطْرَحُوهُ أَرْضاً ** (9) 'Kill Joseph, or cast him away in some land,' [Meaning] unknown, far from civilization (العمران), as is understood from its indefiniteness (تنكيرها) and its being devoid of description (الوصف). يَخْلُ لَكُمْ وَجْهُ أَبِيكُمْ ** 'so that your father's attention may be exclusively for you,' His attention (وجهه) will be purely (يصفُ) (١) for you, so he will turn toward you completely (بكلّيّة), and he will not turn away from you toward anyone else, nor will he dispute with you regarding the love of anyone. وَتَكُونُوا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ ** 'and thereafter you will be' After Joseph, or after his killing. قَوْماً صالِحِينَ ** 'a righteous lot.' Repenting to Allah for what you have committed. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): meaning, you will repent (تتوبون).

(١٠) The Almighty's saying: قالَ قائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ ** (10) A speaker among them said: It was said that he was Yahudha (يهودا), and he was the best of them in opinion. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: He is Lawi (لاوي), narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him), as will follow: لا تَقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ ** 'Do not kill Joseph, for killing is a grave matter. وَأَلْقُوهُ فِي غَيابَتِ الْجُبِ ** but cast him into the bottom of the well, meaning, in the bottom (قعر) of the well (البئر). And it was recited (وقرئ) as غابات. يَلْتَقِطْهُ ** so that some meaning, he takes him (يأخذه). بَعْضُ السَّيَّارَةِ ** of the caravans may pick him up, some of those who travel (يسيرون) upon the earth. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فاعِلِينَ ** if you are going to do it.' [meaning, if you are going to do] that which separates him from his father.

(١١) قالُوا يا أَبانا ما لَكَ لا تَأْمَنَّا عَلى يُوسُفَ ** (11) They said, 'O our father! What is the matter with you that you do not trust us with Joseph? Why do you fear us concerning him? وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَناصِحُونَ ** and indeed we are his well-wishers.' And we are compassionate towards him and desire good for him.

footnote (١) And (*صفا الماء صفواً*) from the pattern (*باب*) of (*قعد*) and (*صفاءً*) with a long *alif* (*ممدوداً*) means when it is purified from turbidity (*الكدر*).

(١٢) أَرْسِلْهُ مَعَنا غَداً ** (12) Send him with us tomorrow إلى الصحراءِ يَرْتَعْ ** (12) so that he may graze to the desert يَرْتَعْ (so that he may graze) meaning: he may expand in eating fruits and other things, derived from الرتعة (al-rut‘ah), which is abundance (الخصب). وَيَلْعَبْ ** (12) and play, by racing (بالاستباق) with feet and throwing (والرَّمي). وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ. (12) and we will indeed guard him. (١٣) قالَ إِنِّي لَيَحْزُنُنِي أَنْ تَذْهَبُوا بِهِ ** (13) He said, 'It grieves me that you should take him away, due to the severity of his separation from me and my lack of patience without him. وَأَخافُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَهُ الذِّئْبُ ** (and I fear that the wolf may eat him). It was said: because the land was infested with wolves (مذأبة). وَأَنْتُمْ عَنْهُ غافِلُونَ. (13) and I fear that the wolf may eat him while you are oblivious of him.' (١٤) قالُوا لَئِنْ أَكَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ ** (14) They said, 'If the wolf eats him while we are a band, meaning: a strong group (جماعة أقوياء) إِنَّا إِذاً لَخاسِرُونَ. (14) then we will indeed be losers!' In al-Majma‘, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace): "Do not prompt (تلقّنُوا) falsehood, lest you lie, for the sons of Jacob did not know that the wolf eats humans until their father prompted them." In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Jacob provided them with the excuse (العلّة), so they used it as an excuse (اعتلّوا بها) regarding Joseph." In al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Jacob was afflicted with the trial of Joseph because he slaughtered a fat ram, and one of his companions was needy and could not find anything with which to break his fast (يفطر عليه). So he neglected him and did not feed him. Thus, he was afflicted with the trial of Joseph. After that, every morning his caller would announce: 'Whoever is not fasting, let him attend Jacob's lunch.' And when the evening came, he would announce: 'Whoever is fasting, let him attend Jacob's dinner.'" In al-Majma‘, al-Ilal, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): A similar account with elaboration (ببَسط) and detail (وتفصيل).

(١٥) فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا بِهِ وَأَجْمَعُوا أَنْ يَجْعَلُوهُ فِي غَيابَتِ الْجُبِ ** (15) When they took him away and resolved to put him in the bottom of the well, And they resolved (عزموا) to cast him into it. The answer [to the لما] is omitted, meaning: they did to him what they did.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When they left their home, their father quickly caught up with them, snatched him from their hands, embraced him, hugged him, and wept. Then he handed him over to them. So they quickly went away with him, fearing that he might take him back from them and not return him. When they were certain of having him, they brought him to a thicket (غيضة) (1) of trees and said: "We will slaughter him and cast him beneath this tree so that the wolf may eat him tonight." Their eldest said: لا تَقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ وَأَلْقُوهُ فِي غَيابَتِ الْجُبِّ يَلْتَقِطْهُ بَعْضُ السَّيَّارَةِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فاعِلِينَ ** their eldest said, 'Do not kill Joseph, but cast him into the bottom of the well, so that some caravan may pick him up, if you are intent on doing [something].' So they went with him to the well and cast him into it, believing that he would drown in it. When he reached the bottom of the well, he called out to them: "O sons of Reuben (رومين), convey my greetings of peace (السلام) to Jacob." When they heard his speech, some of them said to others: "Do not leave this place until you know that he has died." So they remained near him until they despaired and returned.

And al-Qummi [narrates]: So they brought him close to the mouth of the well and said to him: "Take off your shirt." He wept and said: "O my brothers, are you stripping me naked?" One of them drew a knife against him and said: "If you do not take it off, I will surely kill you." So he took it off, and they lowered him into the water (اليم), and they moved away from him. Joseph then said while in the well: "O God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, have mercy on my weakness, my lack of resourcefulness, and my youth."

Then al-Qummi said—and Ibn Tawus attributed this saying to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): His brothers returned and said: "We will take his shirt and smear it with blood and tell our father that the wolf ate him." Their brother Levi (لاوي) said to them: "O people, are we not the sons of Jacob, the Israel of Allah, the son of Isaac, the Prophet of Allah, the son of Abraham, the Friend (خليل) of Allah? Do you think that Allah will conceal this news from His Prophets?" They asked: "And what is the solution (الحلية)?" They said: "We will stand up, perform ghusl (نغتسل), pray in congregation (جماعة), and supplicate to Allah to conceal this from His Prophets, for He is Generous (جواد) and Noble (كريم)." So they stood up and performed ghusl. It was the tradition (سنّة) of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob that they would not pray in congregation until they reached eleven men, one of whom would be the Imam and ten would pray behind him. They said: "How shall we proceed when we do not have an Imam?" Levi (لاوي) said: "We shall make Allah our Imam." So they prayed, supplicated, and wept, saying: "O Lord, conceal this from us."

وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْهِ ** And We revealed to him: Allah Almighty revealed to him in his youth, just as He revealed to John (يحيى) and Jesus (عيسى).

لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُمْ بِأَمْرِهِمْ هذا ** 'You will surely inform them of this deed of theirs,** You will surely tell them what they did to you.

وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** while they are unaware.' [Meaning] that they will not realize that you are Joseph, due to your elevated status and the long passage of time which alters appearances. This is a reference to what He said to them in Egypt when they entered upon Him seeking provisions: فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ مُنْكِرُونَ ، ** He recognized them, but they did not recognize him. He gave him glad tidings of what his affair would eventually lead to, in order to comfort Him and soothe His heart.

Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: "They will not realize that you are Joseph." Gabriel (جبرئيل) came to him and informed him of that.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was seven years old.

(1) Al-Ghayḍah (الغيضة) with a fatḥah (بالفتح) is the thicket (الأجمة) and a gathering place of trees in a water depression (مغيض ماء), or it is specific to the willow (الغرب) and not every tree. Its plural is ghiyāḍ (غياض) and aghyaḍ (اغياض).

(١٦) وَجاؤُ أَباهُمْ عِشاءً ** The end of the day يَبْكُونَ ** feigning weeping. **وَجاؤُ أَباهُمْ عِشاءً يَبْكُونَ (16) ** (16) And they came to their father at nightfall, weeping.

They said: يا أَبانا إِنَّا ذَهَبْنا نَسْتَبِقُ (O our father! We went racing) — meaning, we were competing in running (نتسابق في العَدْو) — وَتَرَكْنا يُوسُفَ عِنْدَ مَتاعِنا فَأَكَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ (and left Joseph with our belongings, and the wolf ate him.) وَما أَنْتَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ لَنا (And you will not believe us) — meaning, you will not confirm us (بمصدّق لنا) — وَلَوْ كُنَّا صادِقِينَ ** (even if we speak the truth) due to your ill-opinion of us and your excessive love for Joseph. قالُوا يا أَبانا إِنَّا ذَهَبْنا نَسْتَبِقُ وَتَرَكْنا يُوسُفَ عِنْدَ مَتاعِنا فَأَكَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ وَما أَنْتَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ لَنا وَلَوْ كُنَّا صادِقِينَ (17) ** (17) They said, 'O our father! We went racing and left Joseph with our belongings, and the wolf ate him. And you will not believe us, even if we speak the truth.'

(١٧) وَجاؤُ عَلى قَمِيصِهِ بِدَمٍ كَذِبٍ ** (17) And they came with false blood on his shirt. —meaning, blood that was lied about (مكذوب فيه). The verbal noun (المصدر) is used as a description for emphasis (للمبالغة). Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "They slaughtered a kid (جدياً) upon his shirt." And al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the shirt of Yusuf was brought to Ya’qub, he said: "O Allah, that wolf must have been a gentle companion (رفيقاً) since it did not tear the shirt." He (the narrator) said: And there was upon it [a splash (نضح) or a stain (فضح)—variant reading (١)] of blood. And al-Qummi said: "How intense was the anger of that wolf towards Yusuf (peace be upon him), yet how compassionate it was towards his shirt, since it ate Yusuf but did not tear his shirt!" قالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنْفُسُكُمْ أَمْراً ** He said, 'Rather your souls have prompted you to [do] a deed.' —meaning, it made easy for you and lightened in your eyes a great matter, derived from al-sawl (السّول), which is relaxation (الاسترخاءِ). فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ** So [mine is] a patience that is graceful. —meaning, my affair is graceful patience. And in the Prophetic Hadith صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Graceful patience (الصبر الجميل) is that in which there is no complaint to the creation." Ibn Uqdah narrated this from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and al-Ayyashi narrated it from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him). وَاللهُ الْمُسْتَعانُ عَلى ما تَصِفُونَ ** And Allah is the One whose help is sought against what you describe.' —meaning, against enduring what you describe concerning the destruction of Yusuf. In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When he heard their statement, he recited istirja' (استرجع) and wept (استعبر). He recalled what Allah had revealed to Him concerning preparation for affliction (البلاء) and submitted to the trial (البلوى)—meaning, due to his negligence in feeding the hungry neighbor. Then he said to them: **بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنْفُسُكُمْ أَمْراً ** (Rather your souls have prompted you to [do] a deed.) "And Allah would not feed the flesh of Yusuf to the wolf before I see the interpretation of his truthful dream."

(١٨) وَجاءَتْ سَيَّارَةٌ ** (And there came a caravan) A company (رُفْقَة) traveling. They dismounted near the well (الجُبّ). فَأَرْسَلُوا وارِدَهُمْ ** (and they sent their waterman) The one who goes to the water (يَرِدُ الْمَاءَ) and draws water for them (وَيَسْتَسْقِي لَهُمْ). فَأَدْلى دَلْوَهُ ** (and he let down his bucket.) He sent it down into the well (الْجُبّ) to fill it. Then Joseph (يوسف) clung to it (فَتَدَلَّى بِهَا). When he (the waterman) saw him, قالَ يا بُشْرى هذا غُلامٌ ** (He said, 'Good news! This is a boy!') He gave good news (بَشَّرَ) to himself or to his people. It was also recited (وَقُرِئَ) as يا بُشْرَايَ with the addition (بِالْإِضَافَة) [of the يَاء of the first person]. وَأَسَرُّوهُ بِضاعَةً (And they concealed him as merchandise.) They hid him (أَخْفَوْهُ) as goods (مَتَاعًا) for trade (لِلتِّجَارَة). This refers either to the waterman (الْوَارِد) and his companions from the rest of the company (الرُّفْقَة), or to Joseph’s brothers (إِخْوَة يُوسُف) from the entire company (الرُّفْقَة جَمِيعًا). وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ ** (And Allah is knowing of what they did.) Their secrets (أَسْرَارُهُمْ) were not hidden from Him.

(١٩) وَشَرَوْهُ بِثَمَنٍ بَخْسٍ ** (diminished, deficient) دَراهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ ** (few). They used to weigh the large amounts and count the small amounts. وَكانُوا فِيهِ ** (regarding Joseph) مِنَ الزَّاهِدِينَ ** (those who were averse to him).

وَشَرَوْهُ بِثَمَنٍ بَخْسٍ دَراهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ وَكانُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الزَّاهِدِينَ (And they sold him for a paltry price—counted dirhams—and they were among the indifferent ones regarding him.)

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was twenty dirhams. And in al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), the like of it, and He added: al-Bakhs (البخس) is deficiency (النقص), and it is the price of a hunting dog if it is killed.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was eighteen dirhams. And in al-Qummi: the like of it.

And in al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When they awoke, they said: "Let us go until we see what the condition of Joseph is—is he dead or alive?" When they reached the well (الجبّ), they found a caravan (سيّارة) present at the well, and they had فَأَرْسَلُوا وارِدَهُمْ فَأَدْلى دَلْوَهُ ** (So they sent their water-drawer, and he let down his bucket). When he pulled up his bucket, behold, there was a boy clinging to his bucket. He said to his companions: يا بُشْرى هذا غُلامٌ ** (O good news! This is a boy!). When they brought him out, Joseph's brothers approached them and said: "This is our slave who fell from us into this well yesterday, and we came today to take him out." So they snatched him from their hands and withdrew with him to one side. They said: "Either you admit to us that you are our slave so that we may sell you to some of this caravan, or we will kill you." Joseph said to them: "Do not kill me, and do whatever you wish." So they brought him to the caravan and said: "Who among you will buy this boy from us?" A man among them bought him for twenty dirhams, and his brothers were among the indifferent ones (الزاهِدين) regarding him.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Joseph's brothers threw Joseph into the well (الجبّ), Gabriel (جبرئيل) came to him and entered upon him. He said: "O boy, what are you doing here?" He replied: "My brothers threw me into the well." He asked: "Do you wish to leave it?" He replied: "That is up to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic; if He wills, He will bring me out." He said: "Then He said to him: 'Indeed, Allah says to you: Call upon Me with this supplication (الدعاء) so that I may bring you out of the well.'" He asked him: "And what is the supplication (الدعاء)?" He replied: "Say: O Allah, I ask You by the fact that all praise belongs to You, there is no god but You, the Bestower (المنّان), the Originator of the heavens and the earth (بديع السماوات والأرض), Possessor of Majesty and Honor (ذو الجلال والإكرام), that You bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد), and that You grant me relief (فرجاً) and a way out (مخرجاً) from what I am in."

And al-Qummi added: "And provide for me from where I reckon and from where I do not reckon." So he supplicated to his Lord, and He granted him relief (فرجاً) from the well (الجب), and a way out (مخرجاً) from the woman's plot (كيد المرأة), and He granted him the sovereignty of Egypt (مُلك مصر) from where he did not reckon.

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi: what is similar in meaning.

And in al-Majalis, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked what Joseph's supplication (دعاء) was in the well (الجبّ), for "we have differed concerning it." He replied: When Joseph was in the well (الجبّ) and despaired of life, he said: "O Allah, if sins and transgressions have worn out my face (أخلقت وجهي) in Your sight, so that You will not raise a voice for me to You, nor respond to a supplication (دعوةً) from me, then I ask You by the right of the Elder Ya'qub (Jacob) (peace be upon him), so have mercy on his weakness and unite me with him, for You certainly know his compassion toward me and my longing for him."

Al-Qummi: So they carried Joseph to Egypt (مصر) and sold him to the Aziz (عزيز) of Egypt.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He was asked how far the distance was between the dwelling of Ya'qub (Jacob) on that day and Egypt (مصر). He replied: A journey of twelve days.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith where Joseph is mentioned: The distance between him and his father was a journey of eighteen days. He said: And Ya'qub (Jacob) and his children traveled upon receiving the good news, a journey of nine days from their desert dwelling (بدوهم) to Egypt (مصر).

footnote (١) *Al-Fadh* (*الفضح*) with a *harakah* (vowel mark) is that which is covered by redness.

(٢٠) وَقالَ (١) الَّذِي اشْتَراهُ مِنْ مِصْرَ ** (20) And he who bought him from Egypt said It is said that he was al-Aziz (العزيز), who was in charge of the treasuries of Egypt, and his name was Qitfir (قطفير) or Itfir (اظفير). The king at that time was Rayyan ibn al-Walid al-Amaliqi (ريّان بن الوليد العمليقي), and he had believed in Yusuf and died during his lifetime. [He said this] to his wife, whose name was Zulaykha (زليخا), as will come from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): أَكْرِمِي مَثْواهُ ** 'Give him an honourable place [to live] Make his dwelling with us honorable (كريماً), meaning good (حسناً). The meaning is: take good care of him. عَسى أَنْ يَنْفَعَنا (perhaps he will benefit us) in our estates (ضياعنا) and our wealth (أموالنا), and we may seek strength through him (نستظهر به) in our affairs (مصالحنا), أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُ وَلَداً ** or take him as a son.' We adopt him (نتبنّاه). That was because of the maturity (الرّشد) he discerned in him. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): And he had no child, so they honored him and raised him. When he reached his full strength (أشدّه), the wife of al-Aziz (امرأة العزيز) desired him (هَوَته). No woman looked at Yusuf except that she desired him, and no man except that he loved him. His face was like the moon on the night of the full moon (ليلة البدر).

وَكَذلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلِنُعَلِّمَهُ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحادِيثِ وَاللهُ غالِبٌ عَلى أَمْرِهِ ** (21) Thus did We establish Yusuf in the land, and that We might teach him the interpretation of dreams. Allah is predominant over His command, He is not prevented from what He wills. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** but most people do not know. the subtleties of His craftsmanship and that the entire affair is in His Hand.

footnote (١) And the implied meaning (*تَقْدِير*) of the verse is: "So they carried him to Egypt and sold him," and that was omitted because the context indicates it.

(٢٢) وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ (22) When he came of age, the peak of the strengthening of his body and his power, آتَيْناهُ حُكْماً We gave him judgment (حكمة) وَعِلْماً وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** and knowledge, and thus do We reward the virtuous. This is an alert that He, the Almighty, only gave him that as a reward for his excellence (احسانِه) in his work and his piety (اتقائه) in the prime of his life.

(٢٣) وَراوَدَتْهُ الَّتِي هُوَ فِي بَيْتِها عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** (23) She, in whose house he was, sought to seduce him. She sought from him and contrived (تمحّلت) that he should lie with her, derived from (راد يرود) when one comes and goes seeking something. وَغَلَّقَتِ الْأَبْوابَ وَقالَتْ هَيْتَ لَكَ ** She bolted the doors and said, 'Come on, you!' Meaning: "Come forward and hasten." It was recited with dammah (بالضّمّ), and with fathah (بالفتح) and kasrah of the hā’ (وكسر الهاء). And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): [It was recited] with the hamzah (بالهمزة) and dammah of the tā’ (وضمّ التّاء), meaning: "I am ready for you (تهيّأت لك)." قالَ مَعاذَ اللهِ ** He said, 'Allah forbid! I seek refuge in Allah, a true refuge (معاذاً). إِنَّهُ رَبِّي أَحْسَنَ مَثْوايَ ** Indeed, He is my Lord, who has given me a good lodging. My master Qitfir has treated me well (أحسن تعهدي), so it is not his reward that I should betray him concerning his family. And indeed, Allah is my Creator (خالقي) and He has elevated my status (أحسَنَ منزلتي) by inclining his heart towards me, so I shall not disobey Him. إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.'

(٢٤) وَلَقَدْ هَمَّتْ بِهِ ** (24) She certainly desired him, She intended to mix with him [have intercourse with him]. وَهَمَّ بِها لَوْ لا أَنْ رَأى بُرْهانَ رَبِّهِ ** and he would have desired her had he not seen the proof of his Lord. Its meaning is: had he not seen the proof of his Lord, he would have desired her (لَهَمَّ بِهَا). The answer (جَوَاب) to لَوْ لَا (if not) was omitted because the previously mentioned text indicates it. This is according to those who do not permit the predicate (الْجَزَاء) to precede the condition (الشَّرْط). As for those who permit it, they have no need for this estimation (التَّقْدِير).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The proof (الْبُرْهَان) is the Prophethood (النُّبُوَّة) which prevents the commission of indecencies (الْفَوَاحِش), and the wisdom (الْحِكْمَة) which turns one away from abominations (الْقَبَائِح). كَذلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ السُّوءَ وَالْفَحْشاءَ إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** Thus [it was], that We might turn away from him all evil and indecency. He was indeed one of Our exclusive servants. Those whom Allah made sincere (أَخْلَصَهُمْ) for His obedience. It was also recited with a kasrah on the lām (الْمُخْلِصِينَ), meaning: those who made their religion sincere (أَخْلَصُوا) for Allah.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), when al-Ma’mun asked him about the infallibility (عِصْمَة) of the Prophets: لَقَدْ هَمَّتْ بِهِ (She certainly desired him) and لَوْ لا أَنْ رَأى بُرْهانَ رَبِّهِ (had he not seen the proof of his Lord), he would have desired her (لَهَمَّ بِهَا) just as she desired him. But he was infallible (مَعْصُوم), and the infallible one does not desire a sin (لَا يَهُمُّ بِذَنْب) nor commit it?

He said: And my father narrated to me from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: She desired to act, and he desired not to act. And in one narration: She desired the disobedience (الْمَعْصِيَة), and Joseph desired to kill her if she forced him, due to the magnitude of what entered his heart. So Allah turned away from him the killing and the indecency (الْفَاحِشَة). This is His saying, the Almighty: كَذلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ السُّوءَ وَالْفَحْشاءَ , meaning: the killing and the fornication (الزِّنَا).

And from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The wife of al-Aziz stood up to the idol (الصَّنَم) and threw a cloth over it. Joseph said to her: "Do you feel shame before one who neither hears, nor sees, nor understands, nor eats, nor drinks, yet I should not feel shame before the One Who created man and taught him?" That is His saying, the Almighty: لَوْ لا أَنْ رَأى بُرْهانَ رَبِّهِ.

And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), after [He said]: The saying of the people that he saw Jacob biting his finger is false.

And al-Qummi also narrated: her standing up to the idol (الصَّنَم), from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And in al-Majalis, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The contentment of people (رِضَا النَّاس) cannot be possessed, and their tongues cannot be controlled. How can you be safe from those from whom the Prophets of Allah, His Messengers, and the Proofs (حُجَج) of Allah were not safe? Did they not attribute to Joseph that he desired fornication (الزِّنَا)?

I say: The common people (الْعَامَّة), may Allah forsake them, have attributed matters to Joseph in this context and narrated various reports concerning them which are not fitting for a believer to transmit, let alone believe. Excellent is what has been said: those who are connected to this incident are Joseph, the woman, her husband, the women [of the city], the witnesses, the Lord of the Worlds, and Iblis. All of them affirmed Joseph's innocence (بَرَاءَة) from sin. Thus, there remains no reason for a Muslim to hesitate regarding this matter.

As for Joseph, [it is] his saying: هِيَ راوَدَتْنِي عَنْ نَفْسِي (She sought to seduce me) and His saying: رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ ** (My Lord! The prison is dearer to me than that to which they invite me.) And as for the woman, [it is] her saying: وَلَقَدْ راوَدْتُهُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ فَاسْتَعْصَمَ ** (Certainly I sought to seduce him, but he was continent.) And she said: الْآنَ حَصْحَصَ الْحَقُّ أَنَا راوَدْتُهُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** (Now the truth has become manifest. I sought to seduce him,) And as for her husband, [it is] his saying: إِنَّهُ مِنْ كَيْدِكُنَّ إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَظِيمٌ ** (This is indeed [a part] of your guile. Your guile is indeed great.) And as for the women [of the city], [it is] their saying: امْرَأَتُ الْعَزِيزِ تُراوِدُ فَتاها عَنْ نَفْسِهِ قَدْ شَغَفَها حُبًّا إِنَّا لَنَراها فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ (The wife of al-Aziz seeks to seduce her slave boy. Her love for him has indeed pierced her heart. We indeed see her in manifest error) and their saying: حاشَ لِلَّهِ ما عَلِمْنا عَلَيْهِ مِنْ سُوءٍ ** (Allah forbid! We know of no evil on his part.) And as for the witnesses, [it is] His saying, the Almighty: شَهِدَ شاهِدٌ مِنْ أَهْلِها ** (A witness from her own family testified.) the Ayah. And as for the testimony of Allah concerning that, [it is] the saying of Him, Mighty is He Who speaks: كَذلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ السُّوءَ وَالْفَحْشاءَ إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (Thus [it was], that We might turn away from him all evil and indecency. He was indeed one of Our exclusive servants.) And as for the acknowledgment (إِقْرَار) of Iblis concerning that, [it is] his saying: فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ إِلَّا عِبادَكَ مِنْهُمُ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (By Your might, I will surely mislead them all, except Your exclusive servants among them.) Thus, he acknowledged that he cannot mislead the exclusive servants (الْعِبَاد الْمُخْلَصِين). And Allah, the Almighty, has said: إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (He was indeed one of Our exclusive servants.) Therefore, Iblis has acknowledged that he did not mislead him. And based on this, we say that these ignorant ones (الْجُهَّال) who attributed disgrace (الْفَضِيحَة) to Joseph: if they are followers of the religion of Allah, let them accept the testimony of Allah regarding his purity (طَهَارَتِه); and if they are followers of Iblis and his soldiers, let them accept the acknowledgment of Iblis regarding his purity.

(٢٥) وَاسْتَبَقَا الْبابَ أي تسابقا إليه وذلك أنّ يوسف فرّ منها ليخرُج وأسرعت وراءه لتمنعه الخروج (They raced to the door)—meaning, they raced towards it. That was because Joseph fled from her to exit, and she rushed after him to prevent him from leaving.

وَقَدَّتْ قَمِيصَهُ مِنْ دُبُرٍ اجتذبته من ورائه فانقدّ قميصه والقدّ الشّقّ طولاً والقطّ الشَّقّ عرضاً وَقَدَّتْ قَمِيصَهُ مِنْ دُبُرٍ ** (and she tore his shirt from behind)—she pulled it from behind him, so his shirt was torn. القدّ (al-qadd) is tearing lengthwise (طولاً), and القطّ (al-qaṭṭ) is tearing crosswise (عرضاً).

وَأَلْفَيا سَيِّدَها وصادفها زوجها **وَأَلْفَيا سَيِّدَها لَدَى الْبابِ ** (They found her master by the door)—and her husband met them.

قالَتْ ما جَزاءُ مَنْ أَرادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوءاً إِلَّا أَنْ يُسْجَنَ أَوْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ بادرت إلى هذا القول إيهاماً بأنّها فرّت منه تبرئة لساحتها عند زوجها وما نافية أو استفهاميّة. قالَتْ ما جَزاءُ مَنْ أَرادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوءاً إِلَّا أَنْ يُسْجَنَ أَوْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (She said, 'What is the requital of someone who intended evil for your wife, except that he should be imprisoned or subjected to a painful punishment?')—She hastened to this statement, giving the false impression that she had fled from him, in order to clear her name (تبرئة لساحتها) before her husband. The word ما (mā) is either negative (نافية) or interrogative (استفهاميّة).

(٢٦) قالَ هِيَ راوَدَتْنِي عَنْ نَفْسِي ** (26) He said, 'She sought to seduce me.' She demanded compliance (المواتاة) from me. And He only said that to ward off what she presented to Him of imprisonment and punishment. And had she not lied against Him, He would not have said it. وَشَهِدَ شاهِدٌ مِنْ أَهْلِها And a witness from her family testified. And He was a child (صبيّ) (1) from her family, visiting her, as will come in the narration from al-Sajjad عليه السلام.

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), inspired Yusuf to say to the King: "Ask this child (الصّبيّ) in the cradle, for He will testify that she sought to seduce me." So al-Aziz spoke to the child (لِلصبيّ), and Allah made the child (الصبيّ) in the cradle speak for Yusuf, and He said: إِنْ كانَ قَمِيصُهُ قُدَّ مِنْ قُبُلٍ فَصَدَقَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ If his shirt is torn from the front, she speaks the truth, and He is one of the liars. Because it indicates that she tore His shirt from the front while defending herself, or that He rushed after her and stumbled on His hem, so His collar was torn.

(٢٧) وَإِنْ كانَ قَمِيصُهُ قُدَّ مِنْ دُبُرٍ فَكَذَبَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (27) But if his shirt is torn from the back, she lies, and he is one of the truthful." Because it indicates that she followed him and pulled his garment, thus tearing it. (٢٨) فَلَمَّا رَأى قَمِيصَهُ قُدَّ مِنْ دُبُرٍ قالَ إِنَّهُ مِنْ كَيْدِكُنَ ** (28) So when he saw his shirt torn from the back, he said, "This is indeed part of your guile." [Meaning] from your cunning (حيلتكن). The address is directed to her and to others like her among women. إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَظِيمٌ ** "Indeed, your guile is great." Because it clings to the heart and affects the soul, due to their confronting [others] with it, unlike the guile of Satan, for he whispers it secretly (مُسَارَقَة).

footnote (١) قيل كان الصّبيّ ابن أخت زليخا وهو ابن ثلاثة أشهر م ن. (1) It was said that the boy (الصّبيّ) was the son of Zulaikha's sister, and he was three months old. (م ن).

(29) يُوسُفُ ** (Joseph!) O Yūsuf. أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هذا ** (turn away from this) Conceal it and do not mention it. وَاسْتَغْفِرِي لِذَنْبِكِ ** (and ask forgiveness for your sin) O (زليخا). إِنَّكِ كُنْتِ مِنَ الْخاطِئِينَ ** (Indeed you have been one of the sinners.) [You are] among the sinful people (القوم المذنبين), derived from (خطئ) when one sins intentionally (إذا أذنب متعمّداً). And the masculine form (التذكير) is used for the sake of predominance (للتغليب).

(٣٠) وَقالَ نِسْوَةٌ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ امْرَأَتُ الْعَزِيزِ تُراوِدُ فَتاها عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** (And women in the city said, 'The wife of the ʿAzīz is soliciting her young man.') She seeks sexual relations with her young man. قَدْ شَغَفَها حُبًّا ** (His love has pierced her heart.) It split the shighāf (شغاف) (covering) of her heart, which is its veil, until the love reached her inner heart (فؤادها). Al-Qummi narrates from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He says: His love has veiled her from people, so she perceives none other than him, and the shighāf (الشغاف) is the veil of the heart. And it was read sha'afahā (شَعَفَها) (with the undotted letter (المهملة)), meaning, it burned her, just as the camel is burned with tar when it is treated with it. And he attributed this reading in al-Majma' (المجمع) and al-Jawami' (الجوامع) to the People of the House (peace be upon them). إِنَّا لَنَراها فِي ضَلالٍ ** (Indeed, we see her in manifest error.) [Meaning, deviation] from guidance (الرشد) and a distance from correctness (الصواب), manifest (مُبِينٍ), apparent (ظاهِر). Al-Qummi: And the news spread in Egypt, and the women began to speak of her story, blaming her and mentioning her.

(٣١) فَلَمَّا سَمِعَتْ بِمَكْرِهِنَ ** (31) When she heard of their machinations, (by their backbiting (اغتيابهنّ) and their reproaching (تعييرهنّ)). He only named it a machination (مكراً) because they concealed it, just as the schemer (الماكر) conceals his scheme (مكره). أَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِنَ ** she sent for them inviting them. وَأَعْتَدَتْ لَهُنَّ مُتَّكَأً and prepared for them a repast, (meaning) food and a place for food, as will be narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), for they used to recline (يتّكئون) while eating and drinking out of luxury (تترفاً), and that is why it was forbidden. And al-Qummi (said) مُتَّكَأً means a citron (أترجة), as if it was read with the sukūn (إسكان) of the tā’ (التاء) and the omission (حذف) of the hamzah (الهمزة). وَآتَتْ ** and gave (meaning) she gave. كُلَّ واحِدَةٍ مِنْهُنَّ سِكِّيناً. each one of them a knife. Al-Qummi (said): She sent for every principal woman (امرأة رئيسة), and they were gathered in her house. She prepared a gathering place for them and gave every woman a citron (أترجة) and a knife (سكّيناً), and said: "Cut [them]." وَقالَتِ اخْرُجْ عَلَيْهِنَّ. And she said, 'Come out before them.' Al-Qummi (said): And he was in the house. فَلَمَّا رَأَيْنَهُ أَكْبَرْنَهُ ** When they saw him, they were stunned by him, (meaning) they magnified Him and were awestruck by His surpassing beauty. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I saw in the second heaven a man whose image was the image of the moon on the night of the full moon. I said to Gabriel: 'Who is this?' He said: 'This is your brother Joseph.'" (Meaning, when He [the Prophet] was taken on the Night Journey (أسرى به)). And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that which is close to it. وَقَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَ ** and cut their hands, (meaning) they wounded them with the knives due to the extremity of their astonishment. وَقُلْنَ حاشَ لِلَّهِ ** and said, 'Good heavens! (This is) a declaration of Allah's transcendence (تنزيهاً لله) from the attributes of incapacity (العجز) and an expression of wonder (تعجّباً) at His power to create the like of him. ما هذا بَشَراً ** This is not a human being! because this beauty is not customary for human beings. إِنْ هذا إِلَّا مَلَكٌ كَرِيمٌ ** This is nothing but a noble angel!' because his beauty is above the beauty of human beings, and because the combination of exquisite beauty (الجمال الرايق), surpassing perfection (الكمال الفائق), and ultimate infallibility (العصمة البالغة) is among the characteristics (خواصّ) of the angels.

(٣٢) قالَتْ فَذلِكُنَّ الَّذِي لُمْتُنَّنِي فِيهِ ** (32) She said, 'So that is the one for whose sake you blamed me.' Meaning, he is that Canaanite servant concerning whom you blamed me for being infatuated with him, before you truly conceived of him. Had you conceived of him with what you have witnessed, you would have excused me. وَلَقَدْ راوَدْتُهُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ فَاسْتَعْصَمَ ** 'Certainly I solicited him, but he was continent.' So he refused, seeking continence (العصمة). She admitted this to them when she knew that they would excuse her, so that they might help her in softening his disposition (عريكتِه). (1) وَلَئِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ ما آمُرُهُ لَيُسْجَنَنَّ وَلَيَكُوناً مِنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ 'And if he does not do what I command him, he will surely be imprisoned and will surely be one of the abased ones.' the humiliated ones (الأذلّاء).

(٣٣) قالَ رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ ** (He said, 'My Lord! The prison is dearer to me than that to which they invite me.) Meaning, more preferred (آثر) by me than complying with them, considering the outcome (العاقبة). And the invitation (الدعوة) is attributed to all of them because they threatened him regarding disobeying her (Zulaykha) and beautified compliance (مطاوعتها) for him.

And al-Qummi (narrates): Joseph did not spend the evening in that house before every woman sent (a message) to him, inviting him to herself. So Joseph became distressed (ضجر) in that house. قالَ رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَ ** [the rest of] the Ayah. وَإِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّي ** (meaning, and if You do not turn away from me) كَيْدَهُنَ ** in making that beloved to me and beautifying it for me, (I ask this) by establishing me upon infallibility (بالتثبيت على العصمة), أَصْبُ إِلَيْهِنَ ** (meaning) I shall incline (أمل) towards responding to them or towards themselves, due to my nature (بطبعي) and the requirement of my desire (مقتضى شهوتي). And al-ṣabw (الصبوّ) is the inclination (الميل) towards passion (الهوى). وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الْجاهِلِينَ ** (meaning) among the foolish (السفهاء) by committing what they invite me to.

(٣٤) فَاسْتَجابَ لَهُ رَبُّهُ ** (34) So his Lord answered him. So Allah answered (3) his supplication, which was contained in His saying: وَإِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّي (and if You do not turn away from me) فَصَرَفَ عَنْهُ كَيْدَهُنَ ** So He turned away their guile from him. So He established him with infallibility (العصمة) until he reconciled himself to the hardship of imprisonment and preferred it over the pleasure that entailed disobedience (للعصيان). إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** Indeed, He is the All-hearing, He is the All-hearing of the supplication of those who seek refuge in Him, الْعَلِيمُ ** the All-knowing of their circumstances and what is beneficial for them. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Joseph (peace be upon him) was among the most beautiful people of his time. When Joseph (peace be upon him) reached puberty (راهق), the wife of the King راوَدَتْهُ ** sought to seduce him عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** away from his soul/self. He said to her: "I seek refuge in Allah! I am from a household that does not commit adultery (لا يزنون)." وَغَلَّقَتِ الْأَبْوابَ ** And she bolted the doors She bolted the doors upon herself and said: "Do not fear." And she threw herself upon him, but he escaped (1) from her, fleeing toward the door. He opened it, but she caught up with him and pulled his shirt from behind, tearing it off him. Joseph (peace be upon him) escaped from her in his remaining clothes, وَأَلْفَيا سَيِّدَها لَدَى الْبابِ and they found her husband at the door. She said: ما جَزاءُ مَنْ أَرادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوءاً إِلَّا أَنْ يُسْجَنَ أَوْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ What is the requital of someone who sought to do evil to your wife, except that he should be imprisoned or [receive] a painful punishment? He (the narrator/Imam) said: The King intended to punish Joseph, but Joseph said to him: "By the God of Jacob (يعقوب), I did not intend any evil toward your wife; rather, هِيَ راوَدَتْنِي عَنْ نَفْسِي (she sought to seduce me). Ask this boy which of us sought to seduce the other." He (the narrator/Imam) said: There was a boy from her family visiting her, and Allah made the boy speak to settle the judgment. He said: "O King, look at Joseph's shirt. If it is torn from the front, then he is the one who sought to seduce her. But if it is torn from the back, then she is the one who sought to seduce him." When the King heard the boy's speech and what he recounted, it greatly alarmed him. The shirt was brought, and he looked at it. When he saw it torn from the back, he said to her: إِنَّهُ مِنْ كَيْدِكُنَّ إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَظِيمٌ ** This is indeed part of your guile; your guile is indeed great. And he said to يُوسُفُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هذا ** Joseph, disregard this, "and let no one hear it from you, and conceal it." He (the narrator/Imam) said: But Joseph did not conceal it and spread it in the city until some women among them said: امْرَأَتُ الْعَزِيزِ تُراوِدُ فَتاها عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** The wife of the Aziz seeks to seduce her slave away from his soul/self. This reached her, so أَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِنَ (she sent for them) and prepared food and a gathering for them. Then she brought them citrons (أترج) وَآتَتْ كُلَّ واحِدَةٍ مِنْهُنَّ سِكِّيناً ** and gave each one of them a knife. Then قالَتِ ** she said to Joseph: اخْرُجْ عَلَيْهِنَّ فَلَمَّا رَأَيْنَهُ أَكْبَرْنَهُ وَقَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ وَقُلْنَ ** Come out before them. When they saw him, they were stunned by him, and cut their hands, and said what they said. Then she said to them: This is الَّذِي لُمْتُنَّنِي فِيهِ ** the one about whom you blamed me meaning regarding her love for him. The women left her presence, and each one of them secretly sent word to Joseph, apart from her companions, asking him for a visit. But he refused them and said: إِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّي كَيْدَهُنَّ أَصْبُ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الْجاهِلِينَ ** If You do not turn away their guile from me, I shall incline toward them and become one of the ignorant. So Allah turned away عَنْهُ كَيْدَهُنَ.* their guile from him.

(٣٥) ثُمَّ بَدا لَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما رَأَوُا الْآياتِ ** (35) Then, after they had seen the signs, it occurred to them after they had seen the evidences (الشواهِد) indicating Yusuf’s innocence لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُ حَتَّى حِينٍ (١) that they should imprison him for a while.** And that was because she deceived her husband and compelled him to imprison him for a period until she could see what would become of him, or until people would assume that he was the criminal.

Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding الْآياتِ ** (the signs), [they were] the testimony of the child (الصّبيّ), the shirt torn from the back, and their racing to the door until he heard her pulling him at the door. When he disobeyed her, she did not cease being infatuated with her husband until he imprisoned him.

And from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): The jailer said to Yusuf: "Indeed, I love you." Yusuf said: "Nothing that has afflicted me has afflicted me except from love. If my maternal aunt (خالتي) loved me, she accused me of theft (2). If my father loved me, my brothers envied me. And if the wife of al-Aziz loved me, she imprisoned me." Al-Ayyashi narrates the same, except that he mentioned the paternal aunt (العمة) instead of the maternal aunt (الخالة).

Al-Qummi added: And he complained to Allah while in prison, saying: "O Lord, by what have I deserved imprisonment?" So Allah revealed to Him: "You chose it when you said: رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ ** (My Lord! The prison is dearer to me than that to which they invite me). Why did you not say: 'Well-being (العافية) is dearer to me than that to which they invite me'?"

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The weepers (البكاءون) are five... until he said: As for Yusuf, he wept over Ya'qub until the people of the prison were bothered by him. They said to him: "Either you weep at night and remain silent during the day, or you weep during the day and remain silent at night." So he made a settlement with them upon one of the two options.

And al-Ayyashi narrates from him (peace be upon him): No one wept the weeping of three... until his saying: As for Yusuf, he used to weep over his father Ya'qub while he was in prison, so he made a settlement with them that he would weep one day and remain silent one day.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Jibril came to Yusuf (peace be upon him) while he was in prison and said to him: "O Yusuf, say after every prayer: 'O Allah, grant me relief (فرجاً) and an exit (مخرجاً), and provide for me from where I expect and from where I do not expect.'" And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), is that which is in the meaning of the two narrations.

footnote (١) It was said: for seven years. And it was said: until a time when the woman's story would spread and his news would be cut off from the people. (*م ن*).

footnote (٢) *سرّقه* (accused him of theft), meaning: attributed theft (*السّرقة*) to him. (*ص*).

(٣٦) وَدَخَلَ مَعَهُ السِّجْنَ فَتَيانِ. (36) And two young men entered the prison with him. Al-Qummi (said): They were two slaves belonging to the king; one was his baker and the other was the cupbearer (صاحب الشراب). قالَ أَحَدُهُما إِنِّي أَرانِي ** (One of them said, 'I dreamt that I) meaning: I saw in a dream, and this is a narration of a past state. أَعْصِرُ خَمْراً ** (was pressing wine.') meaning: grapes, which He named by what they would eventually become (بما يؤل إليه). وَقالَ الْآخَرُ إِنِّي أَرانِي أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِي خُبْزاً تَأْكُلُ الطَّيْرُ مِنْهُ (And the other said, 'I dreamt that I was carrying bread on my head, and the birds were eating from it.) Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said regarding أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِي ** (I was carrying on my head): It was a large bowl (جفنة) containing bread. تَأْكُلُ الطَّيْرُ مِنْهُ نَبِّئْنا بِتَأْوِيلِهِ (the birds were eating from it. Inform us of its interpretation.) Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the king commanded the imprisonment of Yusuf in the prison, Allah the Almighty inspired Him with the knowledge of dream interpretation (علم تأويل الرّؤيا). He used to interpret the dreams of the people of the prison for them. And these two young men were admitted into the prison with him on the day he was imprisoned. When they spent the night and awoke in the morning, they said to him: "We saw a dream, so interpret it for us." He said: "And what did you two see?" قالَ أَحَدُهُما إِنِّي أَرانِي ** (One of them said, 'I dreamt that I') —the rest of the Ayah. إِنَّا نَراكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (Indeed, we see you to be one of the virtuous.) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He used to make space in the gathering (يوسّع المجلس), borrow money for the needy, and assist the weak. And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): He used to attend to the sick, seek out the needy, and provide comfort (يوسع) to the imprisoned. And it was said: [It means] one who excels in the interpretation of dreams (تأويل الرّؤيا), meaning, He knows it.

(٣٧) **قالَ لا يَأْتِيكُما طَعامٌ تُرْزَقانِهِ إِلَّا نَبَّأْتُكُما بِتَأْوِيلِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَكُما ** (37) He said, 'No food will come to you as your provision but I will inform you of its interpretation before it comes to you.'

He intended to invite them both to al-Tawhid (monotheism) and guide them both to the straight path before He granted what they requested from Him, just as is the method (طريقة) of the Prophets and the Successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them) in guidance (الهداية) and instruction (الإرشاد). So, He presented what would be a miracle (معجزة) for Him, namely, informing about the unseen (الاخبار بالغيب), in order to demonstrate to them both His truthfulness in the invitation (الدعوة) and the interpretation (التعبير).

ذلِكُما ** meaning, that interpretation (التأويل)

مِمَّا عَلَّمَنِي رَبِّي ** through inspiration (الإلهام) and revelation (الوحي), and it is not of the category of soothsaying (التكهّن) or astrology (التنجمّ).

إِنِّي تَرَكْتُ مِلَّةَ قَوْمٍ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كافِرُونَ. 'That is a part of what my Lord has taught me. (38) Indeed, I have abandoned the creed of a people who do not believe in Allah, and who are faithless to the Hereafter.'

(٣٨) وَاتَّبَعْتُ مِلَّةَ آبائِي إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ** (38) and I follow the creed of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. This is an explanation (تعليل) for what preceded it, a preparation (تمهيد) for the call (الدعوة), and a manifestation that he is from the household of Prophethood (أهلِ بيت النبوّة), so that their desire (the two companions) to listen to him and trust him would be strengthened. ما كانَ لَنا ** It is not right (ما صحّ) for us, the company of Prophets (معشر الأنبياء), أَنْ نُشْرِكَ بِاللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** whatever thing it may be. ذلِكَ ** That (meaning: التوحيد—monotheism) مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ عَلَيْنا ** is part of Allah's favor upon us (through الوحي—revelation) وَعَلَى النَّاسِ ** and upon the rest of mankind, by our sending to guide them and alert them to it. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ ** But most people (those to whom we are sent) لا يَشْكُرُونَ ** do not thank Him for this favor (الفضل) and blessing (النّعمة), so they turn away from it and do not pay attention.

(٣٩) يا صاحِبَيِ السِّجْنِ (39) 'O my two fellow-prisoners!' Meaning: O its inhabitants, or O my two companions in it, like their saying: O thief of the night (يا سارق الليلة). أَأَرْبابٌ مُتَفَرِّقُونَ (Are diverse lords) Diverse (شتّى), numerous (متعدّدة), equal in standing (متساوية الأقدام), خَيْرٌ أَمِ اللهُ الْواحِدُ (better, or Allah, the One) The One Who is unique in divinity (الألوهية), الْقَهَّارُ (the All-subduing?) The Dominant (الغالب), Whom nothing equals, and Whom none besides Him can resist.

(٤٠) ما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلَّا أَسْماءً سَمَّيْتُمُوها أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمْ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ بِها مِنْ سُلْطانٍ (40) What you worship instead of Him are only names which you and your fathers have named. Allah has not sent down any authority for them.

This is an address to both of them (the two prisoners) and to those in Egypt who follow their religion. Meaning: the things you have named deities (آلهِة) are without any proof (حجة) indicating their deservingness of divinity (الإِلهيّة). Rather, you worship them based on what you ascribe to them, so it is as if you worship nothing but the mere names (الأسماء المجرّدة).

إِنِ الْحُكْمُ ** (The judgment belongs to none) regarding the matter of worship إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ** (but Allah), because He is inherently (بالذاتِ) the One deserving of it.

أَمَرَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ ذلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (He has commanded that you worship none except Him. That is the upright religion, but most people do not know.)

Meaning: the truth (الحقّ). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (but most people do not know), so they stumble (يخبطون) in their ignorance (جهالاتهم).

يا صاحِبَيِ السِّجْنِ أَمَّا أَحَدُكُما ** (O my fellow-prisoners! As for one of you) meaning the cupbearer (صاحب الشراب), فَيَسْقِي رَبَّهُ خَمْراً ** (he will serve wine to his lord) just as he served him before. Al-Qummiyy said: Joseph (peace be upon him) said to him: "You will leave the prison and become in charge of the King's drink, and your status with him will be elevated." وَأَمَّا الْآخَرُ ** (and as for the other) meaning the baker (الخبّاز), فَيُصْلَبُ فَتَأْكُلُ الطَّيْرُ مِنْ رَأْسِهِ (he will be crucified, and the birds will eat from his head). Al-Qummiyy said: He had not seen that [dream] and had lied. So Joseph said to him: "The King will kill you and crucify you, and the birds will eat from your brain (دماغك)." The man denied it and said: "Indeed, I did not see that." Then Joseph (peace be upon him) said: قُضِيَ (١) الْأَمْرُ الَّذِي فِيهِ تَسْتَفْتِيانِ (The matter concerning which you sought my interpretation has been decided.) And that is what your affair will lead to. Meaning: It has been decreed and concluded (قطع وفرغ منه), whether you spoke the truth or lied.

footnote (١) And in this is an indication that he (Joseph) used to say that by way of informing about the unseen (*الغيب*) through what was revealed to him, not in the manner that one of us interprets a dream by way of interpretation (*التّأويل*) [m.n.].

(٤٢) وَقالَ لِلَّذِي ظَنَّ أَنَّهُ ناجٍ مِنْهُمَا ** He knew his salvation. اذْكُرْنِي عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ** "Mention my condition to the king (الملك) and that I was imprisoned unjustly, so that he may release me from the prison." فَأَنْساهُ الشَّيْطانُ ذِكْرَ رَبِّهِ ** It is said: So Satan made the companion of the drink (صاحب الشّراب) forget to mention him to his lord, or He made Joseph forget the remembrance of Allah (ذكر اللهِ) until he sought help from other than Him. فَلَبِثَ فِي السِّجْنِ بِضْعَ سِنِينَ. (42) And he said to the one of the two whom he knew would be saved: "Mention me to your lord." But Satan made him forget mentioning him to his lord. So he remained in the prison for several years.**

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Seven years."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Joseph did not turn to Allah in his situation to supplicate to Him. That is why Allah the Almighty said: فَأَنْساهُ الشَّيْطانُ ذِكْرَ رَبِّهِ فَلَبِثَ فِي السِّجْنِ بِضْعَ سِنِينَ (But Satan made him forget mentioning him to his lord. So he remained in the prison for several years).

He said: So Allah revealed to Joseph at that very hour: "O Joseph, who showed you the vision (الرّؤيا) that you saw?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who made your father love you?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who directed the caravan (السيّارة) toward you?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who taught you the supplication (الدعاء) with which you supplicated until He made for you a way out from the well (الجبّ)?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who made for you an escape from the plotting of the woman?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who made the tongue of the infant speak with your excuse?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who averted the plotting of the wife of the Aziz and the women?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Who inspired you with the interpretation of the vision (تأويل الرّؤيا)?" He replied: "You, O my Lord." He said: "Then how did you seek help from other than Me, and did not seek help from Me, nor ask Me to bring you out of the prison? Instead, you sought help and placed your hope in a servant of Mine to mention you to a creature of My creation who is in My grasp, and you did not turn to Me! Remain in the prison for several years due to your sin (بذنبك) of sending a servant to a servant."

And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): He (the narrator) restricted it to some of these points and added that each time, he cried out and placed his cheek on the ground, then said: "You, O my Lord."

And al-Qummi narrated the like of it. And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): Joseph said: "I ask You by the right of my fathers upon You, that You relieve me." So Allah revealed to Him: "O Joseph, what right do your fathers and grandfathers have upon Me? If your father Adam, I created him with My hands, وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي* ** (29) and breathed into him of My Spirit, and lodged him in My Paradise, and commanded him not to approach a tree therein, yet he disobeyed Me and asked Me, so I accepted his repentance. And if your father Noah, I chose him from among My creation and made him a Messenger to them, and when they disobeyed, he supplicated to Me, so I responded to him, and I drowned them and saved him and those with him in the Ark. And if your father Abraham, I took him as a friend (خليلاً) and saved him from the fire and made it cool and safe for him. And if Jacob, I granted him twelve children, then I concealed one from him, and he did not cease weeping until his sight departed, and he sat on the road complaining about Me to My creation. So what right do your fathers have upon Me?" He said: Then Gabriel said to him: "Say, O Joseph: 'I ask You by Your great favor (منّك العظيم) and Your ancient kindness (إحسانك القديم).'" So he said it, and the king saw the vision (الرّؤيا), and his relief was in it.

And in al-Majma', al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the term ended and Allah permitted him to make the supplication for relief (دعاء الفرج), he placed his cheek on the ground, then said: "O Allah, if my sins have worn out my face (أخلقت وجهي) before You, then I turn to You by the faces of my righteous fathers: Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, and Jacob." So Allah granted him relief. It was asked: "Should we supplicate with this supplication (الدعاء)?" He said: "Supplicate with its like: 'O Allah, if my sins have worn out my face (أخلقت وجهي) before You, then I turn to You by Your Prophet, the Prophet of Mercy, Muhammad, and Ali, and Fatimah, and al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, and the Imams (peace be upon them).'"

(٤٣) وَقالَ الْمَلِكُ إِنِّي أَرى سَبْعَ بَقَراتٍ سِمانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجافٌ (١) (43) And the king said, 'I see seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, وَسَبْعَ سُنْبُلاتٍ and seven ears of corn, In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) recited: وسبع سنابل (and seven ears of corn). خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يابِساتٍ ** green, and seven others dry.' And seven dry ones (وسبعاً يابسات) twisted around the green ones until they overcame them. And He dispensed with explaining their condition by mentioning the condition of the cows. يا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ أَفْتُونِي فِي رُءْيايَ ** O assembly! Give me a ruling concerning my dream, Interpret it (عبّروها) إِنْ كُنْتُمْ لِلرُّءْيا تَعْبُرُونَ ** if you are interpreters of dreams.' if you are knowledgeable of its interpretation (تأويلها).

(٤٤) قالُوا أَضْغاثُ أَحْلامٍ ** (44) They said, 'Confused dreams meaning, these are confused dreams (أَضْغاثُ أَحْلامٍ), which are their mixtures (تخاليطها), their falsehoods (أباطيلها), and what occurs from them, whether it be whispering (وسوسة) or self-talk (حديث نفس). It is the plural of ضغث (2). Its origin is what is gathered from the mixtures (اخلاط) of plants and bundled, so it was metaphorically borrowed for the false vision (الرؤيا الكاذبة). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The vision (الرّؤيا) is of three types: glad tidings (بشارة) from Allah for the believer, a warning (تحذير) from Satan, and confused dreams (أضغاث أحلام). وَما نَحْنُ بِتَأْوِيلِ الْأَحْلامِ بِعالِمِينَ ** and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams.' They mean the false dreams (الأحلام الباطلة) specifically, offering an excuse for their ignorance of its interpretation by claiming that it is something for which there is no interpretation.

footnote (١) العجف محرّكة ذهاب السّمن وهو اعجف وهي عجفاء ج عجاف شاذّ لأنّ افعل وفعلاء لا يجمع على فعال لكنّهم بنوه على سمان لأنّهم قد يبنون الشّيء على ضدّه كقولهم عدّوة لمكان صديقة وفعول بمعنى فاعل لا يدخله الهاء وقد عجف كفرح وكرم ق.

footnote (٢) الضّغث بالكسر والفتح قبضة الحشيش المختلط رطبها ويابسها وأَضْغاثُ أَحْلامٍ مثل أضغاث الحشيش يجمعها الإنسان فيكون منها ضروب مجتمعة م.

(٤٥) وَقالَ الَّذِي نَجا مِنْهُما ** (45) And the one of the two who was saved said (He was) one of the two companions of the prison, and he was the cupbearer (الشّرابيّ). وَادَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ ** and he remembered Joseph after a community (جماعة) of time gathered together, meaning, a long period (مدّة طويلة).

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) (أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام): meaning, after a time (وقت). **أَنَا أُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِتَأْوِيلِهِ فَأَرْسِلُونِ ** I will inform you of its interpretation. So send me. meaning, to the one who possesses its knowledge.

(٤٦) يُوسُفُ أَيُّهَا الصِّدِّيقُ ** (46) Joseph, O truthful one! Meaning, so they sent him to Joseph, and he came to him and said to him: يا يُوسُفُ أَيُّهَا الصِّدِّيقُ (Joseph, O truthful one!), meaning, O one who is eloquent (البليغ) in truthfulness (الصّدق). He only said this because he had experienced his circumstances and knew his truthfulness in interpreting his own dream and the dream of his companion. أَفْتِنا فِي سَبْعِ بَقَراتٍ سِمانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجافٌ وَسَبْعِ سُنْبُلاتٍ خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يابِساتٍ ** Give us a ruling concerning seven fat cows which seven lean ones are eating, and seven green ears of corn and [seven] others dry, Meaning, concerning the dream of that [matter]. لَعَلِّي أَرْجِعُ إِلَى النَّاسِ ** so that I may return to the people, (Meaning) I return to the king and those who are with him. لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ so that they may know. [They may know] its interpretation, or your status (مكانك) and your merit (فضلك).

(٤٧) قالَ تَزْرَعُونَ سَبْعَ سِنِينَ دَأَباً ** (He said, 'You will sow for seven years continuously.) meaning, according to your continuous custom (عادتكم المستمرّة). And it was recited with the quiescence (سكون) of the hamzah (الهمزة). فَما حَصَدْتُمْ فَذَرُوهُ فِي سُنْبُلِهِ ** (What you harvest, leave it in the ear,) so that the weevil (السّوس) does not consume it—this is advice (نصيحة) outside the scope of the interpretation (التّعبير). **إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ ** (except a little of what you eat.) during those years.

(٤٨) ثُمَّ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ سَبْعٌ شِدادٌ يَأْكُلْنَ ما قَدَّمْتُمْ لَهُنَ ** (48) Then will come after that seven hard years, which will consume what you have stored up for them, Meaning: their people (أهلهنّ) will eat what you have stored up for their sake. So, it was attributed to them [the years] metaphorically (على المجاز), in conformity (تطبيقاً) between the expression (المعبّر) and the thing expressed (المعبّر به). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He recited ما قرّبتم لهنّ (what you have brought near for them). And al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, what was revealed was ما قرّبتم لهنّ." إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِمَّا تُحْصِنُونَ ** except a little of what you preserve. You preserve (تحرزون) for the seeds of agriculture (الزراعة).

(٤٩) ثُمَّ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ عامٌ فِيهِ يُغاثُ النَّاسُ ** (49) Then there will come after that a year in which the people will be relieved They will be rained upon by the rain (الغيث) or they will be relieved from the drought (القحط) (derived) from relief (الغوث). وَفِيهِ يَعْصِرُونَ ** and in which they will press [grapes and olives]. What is pressed from fruits and crops. And it was read (وقرئ) with the tā' (ت) and the yā' (ي) in the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول), meaning: they will be rained upon, or they will be saved from its pressing (عصره) when He saves them.

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, this reading (القراءة) is attributed to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And al-Ayyashi added that He (peace be upon him) said: "Have you not heard the Almighty's saying: وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ الْمُعْصِراتِ ماءً ثَجَّاجاً. (And We sent down copious water from the rain-laden clouds)?"

And al-Qummi narrated from Him (peace be upon him): A man recited before the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): ثُمَّ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ عامٌ فِيهِ يُغاثُ النَّاسُ وَفِيهِ يَعْصِرُونَ ** (Then there will come after that a year in which the people will be relieved and in which they will press [grapes and olives]), meaning, in the active voice (على البناء للفاعل).

He (peace be upon him) said: "Woe to you! And what will they press? Will they press wine (الخمر)?" The man said: "O Commander of the Faithful, how should I recite it?" He (peace be upon him) said: "It was only revealed as: 'a year in which the people will be relieved and in which they will be pressed (يُعْصَرُونَ)', meaning, in the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول)." Meaning: they will be rained upon after the famine. The proof for this is the Almighty's saying: وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ الْمُعْصِراتِ ماءً ثَجَّاجاً. (And We sent down copious water from the rain-laden clouds).

(٥٠) وَقالَ الْمَلِكُ ائْتُونِي بِهِ ** (50) And the king said, 'Bring him to me.' after the messenger came to him with the interpretation. فَلَمَّا جاءَهُ الرَّسُولُ ** to bring him out, قالَ ارْجِعْ إِلى رَبِّكَ When the messenger came to him, he said, 'Go back to your lord Al-Ayyashi (narrated) implicitly: meaning al-Aziz (the Minister). فَسْئَلْهُ ما بالُ النِّسْوَةِ اللَّاتِي قَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَ ** and ask him about the case of the women who cut their hands.' He delayed answering the king and prioritized the inquiry about the women and the investigation of his situation, so that the innocence of his character might be revealed, and it might be known that he was imprisoned unjustly. He did not expose the wife of al-Aziz despite what she had done to him, out of nobility (كرماً) and adherence to proper conduct (مراعاة للأدب).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I was astonished by Yusuf's (يوسف) nobility (كرمه) and patience (صبره). May Allah forgive Him. When he was asked about the lean and fat cows, had I been in his place, I would not have informed them until I stipulated that they release me from prison. And I was astonished by Yusuf's patience and nobility. May Allah forgive Him. When the messenger came to him, he said, 'Go back to your lord.' Had I been in his place and remained in prison for as long as he did, I would have hastened the response and rushed to the door, and I would not have sought an excuse. Indeed, he was forbearing (حليماً), possessing deliberation (ذا أناة)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from them both عليهما السلام, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Had I been in the position of Yusuf when the king sent to him asking about his dream, I would not have told him until I stipulated that he release me from prison. And I was astonished by his patience regarding the affair of the king's wife until Allah revealed his excuse."

إِنَّ رَبِّي بِكَيْدِهِنَّ عَلِيمٌ ** 'Indeed my Lord is well aware of their guile.'** He sought witness in Allah's knowledge concerning him and concerning the fact that he was innocent of what she had accused him of.

(٥١) قالَ ما خَطْبُكُنَ ** (51) He said, 'What was your business The King said: What is your affair? إِذْ راوَدْتُنَّ يُوسُفَ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ قُلْنَ حاشَ لِلَّهِ ** when you solicited Joseph for his person?' They said, 'Allah forbid! [They said it] in astonishment at his chastity (عفّته) and his purity (نزاهته) from [sinful suspicion (الريبة الزّنية خ ل)] and at Allah's power to create a chaste person like him. And it was read (حاشا). ما عَلِمْنا عَلَيْهِ مِنْ سُوءٍ ** We know no evil of him.' meaning: of sin (ذنب). قالَتِ امْرَأَةُ الْعَزِيزِ الْآنَ حَصْحَصَ الْحَقُ ** The wife of the chief said, 'Now the truth has become manifest. It became established (ثبت) and settled (استقرّ), derived from (حَصحص) the camel when it casts down its calluses (ثفناته) to kneel, or it means it became manifest (ظهر), derived from (حصّ) his hair when it is completely removed (استأصله) such that the skin of his head appears (بشرة رأسه). أَنَا راوَدْتُهُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ وَإِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** I solicited him for his person, and he is indeed among the truthful.' regarding his saying: هِيَ راوَدَتْنِي عَنْ نَفْسِي ** (She solicited me for my person). And there is nothing greater than the testimony of the adversary that his companion is upon the truth and he is upon falsehood.

(52) ذلِكَ ** (That) confirmation is so that the Mighty One (العزيز) may know أَنِّي لَمْ أَخُنْهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ** (that I did not betray him in secret), meaning: in his absence (بظهر الغيب) regarding his sanctity (حرمته).

Yusuf said this when the messenger returned to him and informed him of their words.

وَأَنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي كَيْدَ الْخائِنِينَ (and that Allah does not guide the stratagem of the betrayers)

He does not execute it (لا ينفذه) nor does He make it successful (ولا يسدّده). And in this is an allusion (تعريض) to the wife of the Mighty One (امرأة العزيز) and a confirmation (تأكيد) of his trustworthiness (أمانته).

(٥٣) وَما أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِي ** (53) I do not absolve my soul. Meaning, I do not declare it pure, out of humility before Allah and as an alert that He did not intend by that to purify His soul or to be amazed by His condition, but rather to manifest what Allah bestowed upon Him of infallibility (العصمة) and divine success (التوفيق). إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ (Indeed, the soul is prone to evil) in that it is naturally inclined toward desires (الشّهَوَات). إِلَّا ما رَحِمَ رَبِّي ** except what my Lord has mercy upon. Except at the time of my Lord's mercy, or except the soul upon which Allah has mercy, so He protects it (عصمه) from that. And it is possible that the exception (الاستثناء) is disconnected (منقطع), meaning: but the mercy of my Lord is what averts evil. And sometimes it is said that the two verses are a continuation of the speech of the wife of Al-Aziz, meaning: "That which I said is so that Yusuf may know that I did not lie against him in the unseen (في حال الغيب) and I spoke the truth concerning what I was asked about. Yet, despite that, I do not absolve myself of treachery (الخيانة), for I betrayed him when I accused him and imprisoned him." She intends by this to apologize for what occurred on her part. This interpretation is derived from the words of Al-Qummi, where he said regarding His saying: لَمْ أَخُنْهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ** (I did not betray him in the unseen): meaning, "I do not lie against him now, as I lied against him before." إِنَّ رَبِّي غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** Indeed my Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful.
