He forgives the inclination of the soul and shows mercy to whomever He wills through infallibility (بالعصمة).

(٥٤) وَقالَ الْمَلِكُ ائْتُونِي بِهِ أَسْتَخْلِصْهُ لِنَفْسِي ** (54) The king said, 'Bring him to me, I shall keep him specially for myself.' I shall make him exclusively for myself (اجعله خالصاً لنفسي). فَلَمَّا كَلَّمَهُ So when he spoke to him. So when they brought him and he spoke to him, and [the King] witnessed from him maturity (الرّشد) and trustworthiness (الأمانة), and inferred his intellect from his speech and his trustworthiness from his chastity (عفّته). قالَ إِنَّكَ الْيَوْمَ لَدَيْنا مَكِينٌ ** He said, 'Indeed, today you are with us established [in position], one possessing status and rank (ذو مكانة ومنزلة). أَمِينٌ trustworthy (مؤتمن) over everything.

(٥٥) قالَ اجْعَلْنِي عَلى خَزائِنِ الْأَرْضِ ** (55) He said, 'Put me in charge of the storehouses of the land. meaning: Put me in charge of their affairs, and the land (الأرض) is the land of Egypt (مصر). And al-Qummi said it means the storehouses (الكناريج) (1) and the granaries (الأنابير). إِنِّي حَفِيظٌ ** (Indeed, I am a guardian) — I will guard them against treachery occurring therein. عَلِيمٌ ** (knowing) of the ways of management during times of scarcity (العلل), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). In al-Uyun and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: حَفِيظٌ ** (a guardian) of what is under my hand, عَلِيمٌ ** (knowing) of every language. And he only sought the governorship (الولاية) so that he might use it to implement the rulings of Allah, spread justice (الحق), and place rights (الحقوق) in their proper places. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): May Allah have mercy on my brother Yusuf. Had he not said اجْعَلْنِي عَلى خَزائِنِ الْأَرْضِ (Put me in charge of the storehouses of the land), He would have appointed him immediately, but He delayed that for a year. And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is permissible for a man to praise himself (يزكّي الرجل نفسه) if he is compelled to do so. Have you not heard the saying of Yusuf: اجْعَلْنِي عَلى خَزائِنِ الْأَرْضِ إِنِّي حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ ** (Put me in charge of the storehouses of the land. Indeed, I am a guardian, knowing), and the saying of the righteous servant: وَأَنَا لَكُمْ ناصِحٌ أَمِينٌ (and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you)? In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the affairs came under the control of Yusuf ibn Ya’qub (peace be upon them both), he placed the food in houses (بيوت) and commanded some of his agents. He used to say: "Sell for such-and-such," while the price (السّعر) was fixed. When he knew that the price would increase on that day, he disliked uttering the word "inflation" (الغلاء) with his tongue. So he said to the agent: "Go and sell," and did not name a price for him. The agent went a short distance, then returned to him. He said to him: "Go and sell," and he disliked uttering the word "inflation" (الغلاء) with his tongue. So the agent went, and the first person who came to measure grain (اكتال) arrived. When the measure reached one measure (مكيال) less than what it was yesterday, the buyer said: "Enough for you, I only wanted such-and-such." The agent then knew that the price had increased by one measure (مكيال). Then another person came and said to him: "Measure for me." So he measured. When the measure reached one measure (مكيال) less than what it was for the first person, the buyer said to him: "Enough for you, I only wanted such-and-such." The agent then knew that the price had increased by one measure (مكيال), until it reached one by one. And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a tradition (حديث): Inflation (الغلاء) only occurs due to buyers lying to one another (تكاذب المشترين بعضهم بعضاً). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Yusuf turned to collecting food, and he collected it during the seven fertile years and stored it (فكبسه) (1) in the storehouses (الخزائن). When those years passed and the barren years (السنون المجدبة) (2) arrived, Yusuf turned to selling the food. In the first year, he sold it to them for dirhams (الدراهم) and dinars (الدنانير), until there remained no dinar (دينار) or dirham (درهم) in Egypt (مصر) and its surroundings except that it became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the second year, he sold it to them for ornaments (الحليّ) and jewels (الجواهر), until there remained no ornament (حليّ) or jewel (جوهر) in Egypt and its surroundings except that it became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the third year, he sold it to them for beasts (الدّوابّ) and livestock (المواشي), until there remained no beast (دابّة) or livestock (ماشية) in Egypt and its surroundings except that it became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the fourth year, he sold it to them for male slaves (العبيد) and female slaves (الإِماءِ), until there remained no male slave (عبد) or female slave (أمة) in Egypt and its surroundings except that they became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the fifth year, he sold it to them for houses (الدّور) and real estate (العقار), until there remained no house (دار) or real estate (عقار) in Egypt and its surroundings except that it became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the sixth year, he sold it to them for farms (المزارع) and rivers (الأنّهار), until there remained no river (نهر) or farm (مزرعة) in Egypt and its surroundings except that it became the property (ملكيّة) of Yusuf. In the seventh year, he sold it to them for their very lives (برقابهم), until there remained no slave (عبد) or free person (حرّ) in Egypt and its surroundings except that he became a slave of Yusuf. Thus, he owned their free people, their slaves, and their wealth. The people said: "We have never seen nor heard of a king whom Allah granted such kingship, wisdom (حكماً), knowledge (علماً), and management (تدبيراً) as He granted this king." Then Yusuf said to the King: "O King, what is your opinion regarding the dominion over Egypt and its people that my Lord has entrusted to me? Advise us with your opinion, for I did not reform them only to corrupt them, nor did I save them from calamity only to become a burden (وبالاً) upon them, but Allah saved them through my hands." The King said to him: "The opinion is yours." Yusuf said: "I call Allah to witness, and I call you to witness, O King, that I have emancipated all the people of Egypt, returned their wealth and their slaves to them, and I return to you, O King, your ring, your throne, and your crown, on the condition that you do not proceed except according to my way (سيرتي) and do not rule except according to my judgment (حكمي)." The King said to him: "Indeed, that is my honor (شرفي) and my pride (فخري), that I should not proceed except according to your way and not rule except according to your judgment. Were it not for you, I would not have been strong enough for it, nor guided to it. I have indeed made my authority mighty (عزيزاً) and inviolable (ما يرام). And I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, the One, without any partner, and that you are His Messenger. So remain upon what I have appointed you over." إِنَّكَ الْيَوْمَ لَدَيْنا مَكِينٌ أَمِينٌ. (54) 'Indeed, today you are with us established and trusted.'

(1) (الكرنج) is like (قرطق). It means the shop (الحانوت) or the merchandise (متاع) of the grocer's shop (حانوت البقال). (ق.)

(٥٦) وَكَذلِكَ ** And like that manifest establishment مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** the land of Egypt. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام (peace be upon him): Joseph ruled Egypt and its deserts, and he did not go beyond it to other lands. Another narrative (حديث آخر) concerning this will follow. يَتَبَوَّأُ مِنْها حَيْثُ يَشاءُ ** He settles in its lands wherever he desires, due to his mastery over all of it. And it was recited (وقرئ) as نشاء (We wish) with the ن (nūn). نُصِيبُ بِرَحْمَتِنا مَنْ نَشاءُ ** in this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخرة). **وَلا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** rather, We pay their rewards in full, immediately and eventually.

**وَكَذلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يَتَبَوَّأُ مِنْها حَيْثُ يَشاءُ نُصِيبُ بِرَحْمَتِنا مَنْ نَشاءُ وَلا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (56) And thus We established Joseph in the land, taking up residence therein wherever he wished. We visit with Our mercy whomever We wish, and We do not waste the reward of the virtuous.

footnote (١) كبس البئر والنّهر يكبسهما طمّهما بالتّراب وذلك التّراب كِبس بالكسر ورأسه في ثوبه أخفاه وادخله فيه ق (1) كبس (kabs) the well (البئر) and the river (النّهر): يكبسهما (yakbisuhumā) means filling them up (طمّهما) with earth (بالتّراب). That earth is called كِبس (kibs) with a kasrah (بالكسر). And (كبس) his head in his garment (في ثوبه) means he concealed it (أخفاه) and put it inside it (وادخله فيه).

footnote (٢) روي : أنّ يوسف عليه السّلام كان لا يمتلي شبعاً من الطّعام في تلك الأيّام المجدبة فقيل له تجوع وبيدك خزائن الأرض فقال عليه السّلام أخاف أن أشبع فأنسى الجياع. (2) It is narrated (روي): Joseph عليه السلام (peace be upon him) would not fill himself to satiety (شبعاً) with food during those years of drought (الأيّام المجدبة). So it was said to him: "You go hungry while the treasures of the earth (خزائن الأرض) are in your hand?" He عليه السلام (peace be upon him) said: "I fear that if I become full, I will forget the hungry (الجياع)."

(٥٧) وَلَأَجْرُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ ** (57) And the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who have faith and are Godwary. (They are Godwary of) polytheism (الشرك) and indecencies (الفواحش), due to His greatness and its permanence. (٥٨) وَجاءَ إِخْوَةُ يُوسُفَ ** (58) And Joseph’s brothers came for provisions (للميرة) (1). That was because Canaan was afflicted by the same drought (الجدب) that afflicted the rest of the lands, so Jacob sent his sons, except Benjamin, to Him. فَدَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ مُنْكِرُونَ ** and entered into his presence. He recognized them, but they did not recognize him. Meaning, Joseph recognized them because his concern (همّته) was tied to them, but they did not recognize Him due to the long period (2), their separation from Him at a young age (سنّ الحداثة), their forgetting Him, their assumption that He had perished, the great difference between the state they saw Him in and the state He was in when they left Him, and their lack of contemplation of His features (حلاه) (3) due to awe and veneration (التهيب والاستعظام).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "His brothers did not recognize him due to the awe and might (عزّه) of the kingship."

Al-Qummi (narrated): Joseph commanded that storage facilities (كناريج) be built for Him out of rock, and He plastered them with lime (الكِلس) (4). Then He commanded the sowing of Egypt, and it was harvested, and He gave every person a share, and He left (the grain) in its ear (سنبله), not threshed (لم يدسّه), and placed it in the storage facilities (الكناريج). He did that for seven years. When the years of drought (الجدب) came, He would bring out the ears of grain and sell them for whatever He willed. The distance between Him and His father was eighteen days, and (His father) was in a desert region (بادية). People from all horizons (الآفاق) would travel to Egypt to obtain provisions (ليمْتاروا) of food. Jacob and his children were residing in a desert region (بادية) where there was muql (مُقل) (5). So Joseph's brothers took some of that muql (المقل) and carried it to Egypt to obtain provisions of food with it. Joseph Himself oversaw the selling. When His brothers entered upon Him, He recognized them, but they did not recognize Him, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When Jacob lost Joseph, his grief and weeping over Him intensified until his eyes turned white out of sorrow (ابْيَضَّتْ عَيْناهُ مِنَ الْحُزْنِ), and he became severely needy, and his condition changed. He used to obtain wheat provisions (يمتار القمح) from Egypt twice a year, for winter and summer. And he sent a number of his children with a small amount of merchandise (ببضاعة يسيرة) to Egypt along with a company that departed... (the rest of) the narrative.

(1) It is said: So-and-so provides provisions (يمير) for his family when he carries their sustenance (أقواتهم) to them from outside their land, derived from al-mīrah (الميرة) (with kasrah then sukūn), which is food that a person obtains as provisions (يمتاره), meaning he brings it from one land to another. And mārahum mayran (مارهم ميراً) is from the pattern of bā‘a (باع) with al-mīrah (بالميرة). And al-mītār (الميتار) is the one who brings the provisions (جالب الميرة).

(2) It was said that there were forty years between the time they threw Him into the pit (الجب) and the time they entered upon Him. Therefore, they denied Him (did not recognize Him) because they saw Him sitting on the throne (السّرير) wearing the clothes of kings, and it had not occurred to their minds that He would reach that state. Joseph, however, was expecting their arrival, so He was more certain (in recognizing them).

(3) Al-ḥilyah (الحلية) (with kasrah) means the creation (الخلقة), the form (الصورة), and the description (الصفة).

(4) Al-kils (الكِلس) (with kasrah) is al-ṣārūj (الصّاروج). Al-ṣārūj (الصّاروج) is lime (النّورة) and its mixtures; it is an Arabized word. And ṣarraja al-ḥawḍ taṣrījan (صرج الحوض تصريجاً) (means to plaster the basin).

(5) Al-muql (المقل) (with ḍammah) is frankincense (الكندر) which the Jews use for fumigation (يتدخّن به). It is the gum (صمغ) of a tree. There are Indian (هندي), Arabian (عربي), and Sicilian (صقلّي) varieties of it, and all are beneficial for coughs (السّعال), insect bites (نهش الهوام), hemorrhoids (البواسير), and cleansing the womb (تنقية الرّحم).

(59) وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهازِهِمْ ** (59) And when he furnished them with their provisions, [Meaning:] He prepared them with their equipment and loaded their mounts heavily with what they had come for. The origin of الجهاز (al-jihāz) is the luggage prepared for travel. قالَ ائْتُونِي بِأَخٍ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَبِيكُمْ. he said, 'Bring me a brother of yours from your father.' Al-Qummi narrated: He treated them well in the measure [of grain] and asked them, "Who are you?" They said, "We are the sons of Ya‘qūb, son of Isḥāq, son of Ibrāhīm, the intimate friend (خليل الله) of Allah, Whom Nimrūd cast into the fire, but he was not burned, for Allah made it cool and safe (بَرْداً وَسَلَاماً) for him." He asked, "What has happened to your father?" They said, "A weak old man." He asked, "Do you have a brother besides yourselves?" They said, "We have a brother from our father, but not from our mother." He said, "When you return to me, bring him to me."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): Yūsuf said to them, "It has reached me that you have two brothers from your father. What happened to them?" They said, "As for the elder of the two, the wolf ate him. As for the younger, we left him with his father, and he is protective (ضنين) of him and compassionate (شفيق) toward him." He said, "Indeed, I would like you to bring him with you when you come to seek provisions. ** أَلا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّي أُوفِي الْكَيْلَ (Do you not see that I fill the measure completely)?" [Meaning:] I complete it and do not diminish anything for anyone. ** وَأَنَا خَيْرُ الْمُنْزِلِينَ (and I am the best of hosts). [Meaning:] the best of those who offer hospitality (المضيفين). And he had provided them with the best accommodation and hospitality.

(٦٠) فَإِنْ لَمْ تَأْتُونِي بِهِ فَلا كَيْلَ لَكُمْ عِنْدِي وَلا تَقْرَبُونِ ** (60) But if you do not bring him to me, there shall be no measure for you with me, nor shall you come near me. The phrase وَلا تَقْرَبُونِ ** (nor shall you come near me) means: and do not enter my dwellings (دياري). This is either a prohibition (نهي) or a negation (نفي).

(٦١) قالُوا سَنُراوِدُ عَنْهُ أَباهُ ** (61) They said, 'We will try to persuade his father concerning him, سَنُراوِدُ عَنْهُ أَباهُ ** (We will try to persuade his father concerning him) means: We will strive diligently in requesting him from his father. وَإِنَّا لَفاعِلُونَ ** and we will surely do it.' وَإِنَّا لَفاعِلُونَ ** (and we will surely do it) means: that, and we will not slacken in it.

(٦٢) وَقالَ لِفِتْيانِهِ ** (62) He said to his retainers, وَقالَ لِفِتْيانِهِ ** (He said to his retainers) means: to his young servants (لغلمانه) who were the measurers (الكيّالينَ). And it was read (وقرئ) as لفتيته (to his young men). اجْعَلُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ ** 'Put their merchandise اجْعَلُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ ** (Put their merchandise) means: that is, the price of their food and what they had brought with them. فِي رِحالِهِمْ ** in their saddlebags, فِي رِحالِهِمْ ** (in their saddlebags) means: in their containers (أوعيتهم). He only did that out of generosity (توسيعاً) and favor (تفضّلاً) toward them, and out of loftiness (ترفعاً) from taking the price of the food from them, and fearing that their father might not have what they could return with. لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَها ** so that they may recognize it لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَها ** (so that they may recognize it) means: perhaps they will recognize the necessity (حقّ) of returning it, and the honor (التكرم) of giving two substitutes when they return (إِذَا انْقَلَبُوا) to their families (إِلى أَهْلِهِمْ) and open their containers (أوعيتهم). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ** when they return to their family, so that they may come back.' لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ** (so that they may come back) means: perhaps their recognition of that will invite them to return (الرّجوع).

(٦٣) فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوا إِلى أَبِيهِمْ قالُوا يا أَبانا مُنِعَ مِنَّا الْكَيْلُ ** (63) When they returned to their father, they said, 'Father, the measure has been withheld from us. They meant Joseph's saying: فَلا كَيْلَ لَكُمْ عِنْدِي (there shall be no measure for you with me), because when He informed them of the withholding of the measure (مَنع الكيل) if they did not bring Benjamin, He had indeed withheld the measure from them at that time (حينئذ). فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنا أَخانا نَكْتَلْ ** So send our brother with us, that we may obtain the measure. [Meaning] we will remove the impediment to measuring out the food we need. And it was read yaktal (يَكْتَلْ) with the yā’ (الياءِ), meaning: our brother will obtain the measure (يَكْتَلْ أَخُونَا) so that his obtaining of the measure (اكتياله) may be joined to our obtaining of the measure (اكتيالنا). **وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ ** And we will indeed guard him.' From any harm reaching him.

(٦٤) قالَ هَلْ آمَنُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** meaning, I will not trust you with him إِلَّا كَما أَمِنْتُكُمْ عَلى أَخِيهِ ** Yusuf مِنْ قَبْلُ ** and you had said concerning him إِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ ** then you did not fulfill your guarantee. فَاللهُ خَيْرٌ حافِظاً ** So I rely upon Allah and entrust my affair to Him. وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ He will have mercy on my weakness and my old age, so He will protect him and return him to me, and He will not combine two calamities against me. In al-Majma', in the narration: that Allah, the Glorified, said: "By My Might, I shall surely return them both to you, after you have relied upon Me."

(٦٥) وَلَمَّا فَتَحُوا مَتاعَهُمْ ** (And when they opened their baggage) meaning, the containers (أوعية) of their baggage. وَجَدُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِمْ قالُوا يا أَبانا ما نَبْغِي ** (they found their merchandise returned to them. They said, 'O Father! What more can we desire?) What do we seek? Is there anything more than this? He honored us, treated us well, sold to us, and returned our baggage to us. The meaning is: We do not seek any kindness (إحساناً) beyond that, or: We do not want another merchandise (بضاعة) from you. هذِهِ بِضاعَتُنا رُدَّتْ إِلَيْنا وَنَمِيرُ أَهْلَنا ** (This is our merchandise, returned to us. And we will procure provision for our family,) so we will gain strength through it, and we will procure provision (نَمِيرُ) for our family by returning to the King. وَنَحْفَظُ أَخانا ** (and we will guard our brother,) from dangers during our going and our returning. وَنَزْدادُ كَيْلَ بَعِيرٍ ** (and we will receive an extra camel-load of grain.) meaning, a camel-load (وَسْق بَعِير) by taking our brother along. ذلِكَ كَيْلٌ يَسِيرٌ ** (That is an easy measure.') meaning, a small measure (مَكِيل) that is insufficient for us. They considered what was measured out to them to be little, so they wished to add to it what would be measured out for their brother. Or, they meant that a camel-load (كَيْل بَعِير) is easy (يَسِيرٌ), and the King will not be troubled by us concerning it.

(٦٦) قالَ لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُ مَعَكُمْ ** (66) He said, 'I will not send him with you, إذ رأيت منكم ما رأيت حَتَّى تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقاً مِنَ since I have seen from you what I have seen اللهِ ** until you give me a solemn pledge in the name of Allah حتى [تعطوني تؤتوني خ ل] ما أتوثق به من عند الله أي عهداً مؤكداً بذكر الله لَتَأْتُنَّنِي بِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ يُحاطَ بِكُمْ until you give me [variant reading: تؤتوني] that by which I may secure myself from Allah, meaning, a covenant confirmed by the mention of Allah, that you will surely bring him back to me, unless you are encompassed.' الّا أن تغلبوا فلا تطيقوا ذلك أو الا أن تهلكوا جميعاً فَلَمَّا آتَوْهُ مَوْثِقَهُمْ ** Except that you are overcome (تُغْلَبُوا) and thus unable to do that, or except that you all perish. When they gave him their solemn pledge, عهدهم قالَ اللهُ عَلى ما نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ ** their covenant, he said, 'Allah is witness to what we say.' رقيب مطلع ان أخلفتم انتصف لي منكم. He is a Watcher (رَقِيب) and Aware (مُطَّلِع); if you break the promise, He will take vengeance (انْتَصَفَ لِي) for me from you.

(٦٧) **وَقالَ يا بَنِيَّ لا تَدْخُلُوا مِنْ بابٍ واحِدٍ وَادْخُلُوا مِنْ أَبْوابٍ مُتَفَرِّقَةٍ ** (67) And he said, 'O my sons! Do not enter by a single gate, but enter by different gates.'

Because they possessed beauty, splendor, and a handsome appearance, and they had become famous in Egypt due to their proximity to the king and the special honor (التكرمة) that was not granted to others besides them, so he feared the evil eye (العين) upon them. وَما أُغْنِي عَنْكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ (And I cannot avail you anything against Allah.) Meaning, even if Allah intends harm (سوءً) for you, what I advised you regarding separation (entering different gates) will not benefit you nor repel it from you, and it will inevitably afflict you, for caution (الحذر) does not prevent destiny (القدر). إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَعَلَيْهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ. (The judgement belongs only to Allah. In Him I have put my trust, and in Him let the trusting put their trust.)

(٦٨) وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَهُمْ أَبُوهُمْ ** (68) When they entered in the manner their father had commanded them, meaning, through separate gates (أبواب متفرقة). ما كانَ يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ ** the opinion of Jacob (يعقوب) and their following it مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** it did not avail them in any way against Allah, against what He had decreed upon them, just as Jacob (يعقوب) had said. So they were accused of theft (فسرّقوا), and Benjamin (بنيامين) was seized, and the calamity upon Jacob (يعقوب) was doubled. إِلَّا حاجَةً فِي نَفْسِ يَعْقُوبَ ** but it was a need in Jacob’s soul that He satisfied. This is an interrupted exception (استثناء منقطع), meaning: but a need in his soul, meaning his compassion (شفقته) for them and his caution (حرازته) against them being afflicted by the evil eye (أن يعانوا). قَضاها ** He manifested it (أظهرها) and commanded it (وصىَّ بها). وَإِنَّهُ لَذُو عِلْمٍ لِما عَلَّمْناهُ ** Indeed, he was possessed of knowledge because of what We had taught him, He is possessed of certainty (يقين) and knowledge (معرفة) of Allah because of Our teaching Him, and that is why he said: ما أُغْنِي عَنْكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** (I cannot avail you anything against Allah), and he was not deceived by his own planning (تدبيره). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ but most people do not know. the secret of Divine Decree (سر القدر) and that caution (الحذر) does not avail against it.

(69) وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلى يُوسُفَ آوى إِلَيْهِ أَخاهُ ** (69) When they entered into Joseph’s presence, he lodged his brother with him. He embraced Benjamin (بنيامين) to himself. قالَ إِنِّي أَنَا أَخُوكَ فَلا تَبْتَئِسْ ** He said, ‘I am your brother, so do not grieve So do not grieve from the distress بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (by what they used to do) concerning our right, for Allah has been good to us and has reunited us.

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He had prepared food for them, and when they entered his presence, he said: "Let every son of a mother sit at a table." He said: So they sat down, and Benjamin (بنيامين) remained standing. Joseph (يوسف) said to him: "What is wrong with you that you do not sit?" He said to Him: "You told me to let every son of a mother sit at a table, and I do not have a son of a mother among them." He said: "Did you not have a son of a mother?" Benjamin (بنيامين) said: "Yes." Joseph (يوسف) said: "What happened to him?" He said: "These people claimed that the wolf ate him." He said: "How great was your grief over him?" He said: "Eleven sons were born to me, and I derived a name for each of them from his name." Joseph (يوسف) said to him: "I see that you have embraced women and smelled the scent of children after him." Benjamin (بنيامين) said to him: "I have a righteous father, and he said: 'Marry, perhaps Allah will bring forth from you offspring who will fill the earth with glorification (التسبيح).'" So he said to him: "Come and sit with me at my table." The brothers of Joseph (يوسف) said: "Allah has indeed favored Joseph (يوسف) and his brother, to the extent that the King has seated him with him at his table." And in another narration: When he seated him with him at the table, they stopped eating and said: "We desire one thing, but Allah refuses except to elevate the son of Yamil over us."

And al-Qummi: So they departed, and Benjamin (بنيامين) went out with them, and he would not eat with them, nor sit with them, nor speak to them. When they arrived in Egypt, they entered Joseph's (يوسف) presence and greeted him. Joseph (يوسف) looked at his brother and recognized him. He sat far away from them. Joseph (يوسف) said: "Are you their brother?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Why do you not sit with them?" He said: "Because they took my brother, born of my mother and father, and then returned without bringing him back, claiming that the wolf ate him. So I swore to myself that I would not join them in any matter as long as I live." He said: "Have you married?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Have you had children?" He said: "Yes." He said: "How many children have you had?" He said: "Three sons." He said: "What did you name them?" He said: "I named one of them the Wolf (الذئب), one the Shirt (القميص), and one the Blood (الدم)." He said: "And why did you choose these names?" He said: "So that I do not forget my brother; whenever I call one of my children, I remember my brother." Joseph (يوسف) said to them: "Leave," and he detained Benjamin (بنيامين). When they left his presence, Joseph (يوسف) said to his brother: "I am your brother, Joseph (يوسف). فَلا تَبْتَئِسْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (So do not grieve by what they used to do)." Then he said to him: "I would love for you to stay with me." He said: "My brothers will not allow me, for my father has taken the covenant of Allah and His solemn pledge from them that they would return me to him." He said: "I will devise a trick (حيلة), so do not object if you see something, and do not inform them." He said: "No."

(٧٠) فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهازِهِمْ جَعَلَ السِّقايَةَ ** the drinking vessel (المشربة) فِي رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ ثُمَّ أَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌ ** a caller called out (نادى مناد): أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ ** O caravan (أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ), meaning the caravan (القافلة), and it is the name of the camels upon which the loads are placed, so it was said to their owners. (70) When he had furnished them with their provisions, he put the drinking-cup in his brother’s saddlebag. Then an announcer announced: “O caravan!**

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Its meaning is O people of the caravan (يا أهل العير). Similar to this is their saying to their father: وَسْئَلِ الْقَرْيَةَ الَّتِي كُنَّا فِيها (82) And ask the town where we were, وَالْعِيرَ الَّتِي أَقْبَلْنا فِيها إِنَّكُمْ لَسارِقُونَ ** and the caravan with which we came. You are indeed thieves.”**

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They did not steal, and Joseph did not lie, for He only meant the stealing of Joseph from his father.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Joseph intended reconciliation (الإصلاح).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Speech is of three types: truth (صدق), falsehood (كذب), and reconciliation (إصلاح) between people.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: There is no lie upon one who seeks reconciliation (مصلح). Then he recited: O caravan (أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ), you are indeed thieves (إِنَّكُمْ لَسارِقُونَ). Then he said: By Allah, they did not steal, and he did not lie.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): By Allah, they were not thieves, and he did not lie.

And it was added in al-Ilal (العلل) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ): Do you not see that He said to them when قالُوا وَأَقْبَلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ ما ذا تَفْقِدُونَ قالُوا نَفْقِدُ صُواعَ الْمَلِكِ ** (71) They said, turning to them, “What is it that you miss?” (72) They said, “We miss the king’s drinking-cup, and they did not say: 'You stole the king's drinking-cup (صواع الملك).' He only meant: 'You stole Joseph from his father.'

(٧١) **قالُوا وَأَقْبَلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ ما ذا تَفْقِدُونَ ** (71) They said, turning to them, 'What is it that you miss?' Meaning: What thing is lost from you?

(٧٢) قالُوا نَفْقِدُ صُواعَ الْمَلِكِ ** (72) They said, 'We miss the king's measure.' Meaning: His measure (صاعه) which was previously expressed as the drinking-cup (السقاية), because it was also His drinking vessel (مشربته).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said regarding صُواعَ الْمَلِكِ ** (the king's measure): It is the bowl (الطاس) from which He drinks.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was a golden cup (قدحاً من ذهب), and it was the measure (صواع) of Yusuf; when measuring was done, it was measured with it.

And al-Qummi (said): The measure (الصاع) with which they used to measure was made of gold, and they placed it in his saddlebag (رحله) without his brothers becoming aware of it.

وَلِمَنْ جاءَ بِهِ حِمْلُ بَعِيرٍ ** ...and for him who brings it [there shall be] a camel-load [of grain], of food, as a reward for him, وَأَنَا بِهِ زَعِيمٌ (and I am answerable for it), meaning: a guarantor (كفيل), I will deliver it to the one who returns it.

(٧٣) قالُوا تَاللهِ ** An oath (قَسَم) containing the meaning of astonishment (التعجّب). لَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما جِئْنا لِنُفْسِدَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَما كُنَّا سارِقِينَ ** (you certainly know that we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we have not been thieves.') They sought witness through their knowledge regarding the innocence (براءة) of their souls, because the proofs (دلائل) of their religion (دينهم), their trustworthiness (أمانتهم), the excellence of their conduct (حسن سيرتهم), and their dealings (ومعاملتهم) with them had been established with them time after time (مرّة بعد أخرى).

(٧٤) قالُوا فَما جَزاؤُهُ ** (74) They said, 'What is his penalty What is the penalty for the theft (السرق), or the thief (السارق), or the bowl (الصواع)—meaning the theft of it—by omitting the genitive construct (المضاف) if you are liars in your claim of innocence from it. (٧٥) قالُوا جَزاؤُهُ مَنْ وُجِدَ فِي رَحْلِهِ فَهُوَ جَزاؤُهُ ** (75) They said, 'His penalty is that he in whose baggage it is found shall himself be its penalty.' Meaning, the penalty for his theft is the taking of the one in whose baggage it was found and his enslavement (استرقاقه). This is how the law (شرع) of Jacob (عليه السلام) was. Al-Qummi (said regarding): he in whose baggage it is found: then imprison him (فاحبسه). And Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They meant the custom (السّنة) that was practiced among them, which was that he should imprison him. كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ (Thus do We requite the wrongdoers) (who commit) theft.

(٧٦) فَبَدَأَ بِأَوْعِيَتِهِمْ قَبْلَ وِعاءِ أَخِيهِ ** (76) So he began with their sacks before the sack of his brother. (Benjamin), in order to ward off suspicion. ثُمَّ اسْتَخْرَجَها ** Then he brought it out (meaning, the drinking-cup) مِنْ وِعاءِ أَخِيهِ. from his brother’s sack.

Al-Qummi narrates: So they seized his brother and imprisoned him. كَذلِكَ ** Thus (meaning, like this scheme, We schemed for Joseph by teaching it to him). ما كانَ لِيَأْخُذَ أَخاهُ فِي دِينِ الْمَلِكِ ** He could not take his brother under the king’s law (the king of Egypt), because the ruling for a thief in his law was that he should be beaten and fined, not enslaved. إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ ** unless Allah willed (that He make that ruling the ruling of the king). نَرْفَعُ دَرَجاتٍ مَنْ نَشاءُ ** We raise in rank whomever We wish (through knowledge, just as We raised the rank of Joseph through it). وَفَوْقَ كُلِّ ذِي عِلْمٍ عَلِيمٌ ** and above every man of knowledge there is one more knowing** (who is higher in rank than him in his knowledge).

(٧٧) قالُوا إِنْ يَسْرِقْ فَقَدْ سَرَقَ أَخٌ لَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ. (77) They said, 'If he steals, an earlier brother of his has stolen before.' Al-Qummi said: They meant Yusuf. And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): They meant the belt (المِنطقة). And from him (peace be upon him), he said: "There was a belt (مِنطقة) belonging to the Prophet Ishaq (peace be upon him) which the prophets and the great ones inherited. It was with Yusuf's aunt, and Yusuf was with her, and she loved him. His father sent to her, saying: 'Send him to me, and I will return him to you.' So she sent to him, saying: 'Leave him with me tonight so I may smell him, then I will send him to you tomorrow morning.' When morning came, she took the belt (المِنطقة) and tied it around his waist (حَقْو) (1), dressed him in a shirt, and sent him to him (Ya'qub). And she said: 'The belt (المِنطقة) has been stolen,' and it was found upon him. And when someone stole in that time, he would be handed over to the owner of the stolen item, so she took him, and he remained with her." And in al-Uyun, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi also, narrated from him (peace be upon him), there is a meaning close to this. Likewise, in al-Khara'ij, narrated from Abu Muhammad (peace be upon him), with a more detailed explanation. At the end of it (the narrative), it states: Ya'qub said to her: "He is your slave, on the condition that you neither sell him nor give him away." She said: "Then I accept him on the condition that you do not take him from me, and I will emancipate him this very hour." So he gave him to her, and she emancipated him. فَأَسَرَّها يُوسُفُ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَلَمْ يُبْدِها لَهُمْ ** (77) Yusuf kept it secret in his heart and did not disclose it to them. He concealed it (أَكَنَّها) and did not reveal it to them. He said within himself: أَنْتُمْ شَرٌّ مَكاناً ** (You are worse in position) in rank (مَنْزِلَة) due to your stealing of your brother and your evil deed against him. **وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما تَصِفُونَ ** (and Allah knows best what you allege), and He knows that the matter is not as you describe, and that he did not steal.

footnote (١) *Al-Hiqwu* (*الحَقْو*)—with the unpointed letter (*الحَاء*) having a *fathah* (*فَتْحَة*) and the *qaf* (*قَاف*) having a *sukun* (*سُكُون*)—is the place where the lower garment (*الإِزَار*) is tied, which is the flank (*الخَاصِرَة*). Then they expanded its usage until they called the lower garment (*الإِزَار*) that is tied over the private parts *hiqwu* (*حَقْو*). The plural forms are *huqq* (*حُقّ*) and *hiqiyy* (*حِقِيّ*), similar to *fils* (*فِلْس*), *filsiyy* (*فِلْسَى*), and *fulus* (*فُلُوس*). (M.)

(٧٨) قالُوا يا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ إِنَّ لَهُ أَباً شَيْخاً كَبِيراً ** (78) They said, 'O Aziz! He has an aged, old father. (He is great) in age (السّنّ) or status (القدر). And they mentioned his condition to Him, seeking to evoke His compassion towards him. فَخُذْ أَحَدَنا مَكانَهُ ** so take one of us in his place. For his father is bereaved (1) over his perished brother, and he is comforted by him (Benjamin). إِنَّا نَراكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** Indeed, we see you as one of the virtuous.' Your habit is benevolence (الإِحسان).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding **نَراكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (Indeed, we see you as one of the virtuous), (it means) if you do (this).

(٧٩) قالَ مَعاذَ اللهِ ** (79) He said, 'Allah forbid We seek refuge in Allah, a refuge, أَنْ نَأْخُذَ إِلَّا مَنْ وَجَدْنا مَتاعَنا عِنْدَهُ ** that we should seize anyone except him with whom we found our property! For seizing anyone other than him is an injustice according to your legal ruling (فتواكم). So, if one of you were seized in his place, إِنَّا إِذاً لَظالِمُونَ ** we would then be wrongdoers.' in your view. This is the apparent meaning of his speech. And its inner meaning (باطنه) is that He, the Exalted (تعالى), commanded us to seize Benjamin and detain him for benefits (مصالح) that He knew concerning that matter. So, if I were to seize someone else, I would be an unjust person (ظالماً), acting contrary to what I was commanded.

Al-Qummi narrated: He said: إِلَّا مَنْ وَجَدْنا مَتاعَنا عِنْدَهُ ** (except him with whom we found our property!) and did not say: "except him who stole our property." He said: So they gathered around Joseph and were arguing with him concerning his detention. And when they became angry, hair would emerge from their clothes, and yellow blood would drip from their heads, while they were saying to him: فَخُذْ أَحَدَنا مَكانَهُ إِنَّا نَراكَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** so seize one of us instead of him. We indeed see you to be one of the virtuous.' So, he released from this.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that which is close to it.

(٨٠) فَلَمَّا اسْتَيْأَسُوا مِنْهُ (80) When they despaired of him, They despaired (يئسُوا) of Joseph and of his answering them. The addition of the sīn (السّين) and the tāʾ (التّاءِ) is for emphasis (للمبالغة). خَلَصُوا they withdrew privately. They separated (انفردوا) and isolated themselves (واعتزلوا). نَجِيًّا Conversing secretly (متناجين). Their eldest (كَبِيرُهُمْ) said:

Al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Judah (يهودا) said to them, and he was their eldest. Al-Qummi said: Levi (لاوي) said to them:

أَلَمْ تَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ أَباكُمْ قَدْ أَخَذَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَوْثِقاً مِنَ اللهِ "Do you not know that your father has taken a solemn pledge from you in Allah’s Name, A firm covenant (عهداً وثيقاً). وَمِنْ قَبْلُ ما فَرَّطْتُمْ فِي يُوسُفَ and before that what you neglected regarding Joseph? And before (وَمِنْ قَبْلُ) this (ومن قبل هذا), you fell short (قصرتم) concerning his affair. فَلَنْ أَبْرَحَ الْأَرْضَ I will not leave this land I will not depart from the land of Egypt (أرض مصر). حَتَّى يَأْذَنَ لِي أَبِي until my father permits me, To return to him (في الرجوع إليه). أَوْ يَحْكُمَ اللهُ لِي or Allah judges for me, Or Allah decrees (يقضِي الله) for me to depart (بالخروج). وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحاكِمِينَ and He is the best of all judges." Because He judges only with the truth (بالحق).

Al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

Al-Qummi said: The brothers of Joseph returned to their father, and Judah (يهودا) remained behind. He entered upon Joseph, speaking to him concerning his brother, until the conversation escalated between them, until Judah (يهودا) became angry. There was a hair on his shoulder, and when he became angry, the hair would stand up and continuously spurt blood until one of the sons of Jacob (يعقوب) touched him. He said: There was a small son of Joseph before him, holding a golden pomegranate (رمّانة من ذهب) with which he was playing. When Joseph saw that he (Judah) had become angry and the hair was spurting blood, he took the pomegranate from the boy's hand, then rolled it toward Judah (يهودا). The boy followed it to take it, and his hand fell upon Judah (يهودا), so his anger departed. He said: Judah (يهودا) became suspicious (ارتاب), and the boy returned the pomegranate to Joseph. Then Judah (يهودا) returned to Joseph and spoke to him concerning his brother, until the conversation escalated between them, until Judah (يهودا) became angry and the hair stood up and began spurting blood. When Joseph saw that, He rolled the pomegranate toward Judah (يهودا). The boy followed it to take it, and his hand fell upon Judah (يهودا), so his anger subsided. He said: Judah (يهودا) then said: "Indeed, there is one of the sons of Jacob (يعقوب) with us in the house," until this was done three times.

(٨١) ارْجِعُوا إِلى أَبِيكُمْ فَقُولُوا يا أَبانا إِنَّ ابْنَكَ سَرَقَ ** (81) Return to your father and say, 'O our father, your son committed theft.' based on what we witnessed of the apparent matter (ظاهر الأمر). وَما شَهِدْنا عليه إِلَّا بِما عَلِمْنا ** (And we did not testify concerning him except with what we knew) by seeing that the measure (الصواع) was extracted from his container (وعائه). وَما كُنَّا لِلْغَيْبِ (And we were not of the unseen) guardians of the inner reality of the situation (لباطن الحال). So we do not know whether he stole or the measure (الصاع) was secretly placed (دُسّ) in his baggage (رحله).

(٨٢) وَسْئَلِ الْقَرْيَةَ الَّتِي كُنَّا فِيها ** (And ask the town where we were) Send [a messenger] to its people and ask them about the incident. وَالْعِيرَ الَّتِي أَقْبَلْنا فِيها ** (and the caravan with which we came) And the companions (أصحاب) of the caravan (العير) with whom we traveled and with whom we were. وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ ** (and we are indeed truthful) An affirmation (تأكيد) in the place of an oath (القسم).

(83) قالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ ** (He said, 'Rather, your souls have prompted you to something.) Meaning, so when they returned to their father and told him what their brother had told them, قالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ meaning: adorned (زيّنت) and made easy (سهّلت) for you your souls an affair (أمراً) that you desired, such as your teaching him that the thief is seized for his theft. فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ** (So [mine is] a graceful patience.) So my affair is a graceful patience (صبر جميل), in which there is no complaint to people. عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَأْتِيَنِي بِهِمْ جَمِيعاً ** (Maybe Allah will bring them all to me.) Joseph (يوسف), Benjamin (بنيامين), and Judah (يهودا). إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَلِيمُ (Indeed, He is the All-knowing) of my condition and their condition, the All-wise (الْحَكِيمُ) in managing them.

(٨٤) وَتَوَلَّى عَنْهُمْ ** (And he turned away from them) And he turned away from them. وَقالَ يا أَسَفى عَلى يُوسُفَ ** (and said, 'Alas for Joseph!') [Meaning:] Come, for this is your time (أوانك). And الأسَف (grief) is the most intense sorrow (الحزن) and regret (الحسرة). The ألف (alif) is a substitute for the ياء of the first person singular (ياء المتكلم).

His grieving (تأسّفه) over Joseph without grieving over anyone else is evidence that the loss (فايت) held its proper place (موقعه) with him, and that his affliction (مصابه) concerning him remained fresh and tender (غضّاً طريّاً) despite the long passage of time (طول العهد).

Al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how great Jacob's (يعقوب) sorrow (حزن) for Joseph had reached. He said: "The sorrow of seventy bereaved women (ثكلى) who lost their children." Al-Ayyashi added: It was said to him: "How could Jacob grieve for Joseph when Gabriel (جبرئيل) had informed him that he had not died and that he would return to Him?" He (Imam al-Sadiq) replied to him: "He forgot that." Al-Qummi added: And Jacob did not know the saying of الاسترجاع (i.e., إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ), and it is from this that he said: يا أَسَفى عَلى يُوسُفَ. (Alas for Joseph!).

And in the Prophetic Hadith (الحديث النبوي): No nation among the nations was given إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ ** (Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him do we return) at the time of affliction (المصيبة) except the nation (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Do you not see Jacob (يعقوب) when what afflicted him occurred, he did not say الاسترجاع but rather وَقالَ يا أَسَفى ** (and said, 'Alas!'), [until the end of] the Ayah.

وَابْيَضَّتْ عَيْناهُ مِنَ الْحُزْنِ ** (and his eyes turned white out of grief) due to the abundance of his weeping from sorrow (الحزن), and the tears (العبرة) obliterated their blackness (سوادها).

And al-Qummi means: he became blind (عميت) from weeping. فَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ ** (and he was suppressing his grief) [Meaning:] filled (مملوّ) with rage (الغيظ) against his sons, holding it back (ممسك له) in his heart and not revealing it.

قالُوا تَاللهِ تَفْتَؤُا تَذْكُرُ يُوسُفَ ** (They said, 'By Allah, you will not cease remembering Joseph) Meaning, you will not cease (لا تفتؤا) and you will not stop (ولا تزال) remembering him, grieving over him. The particle لا (not) was omitted because there is no confusion with the affirmative. حَتَّى تَكُونَ حَرَضاً until you become حَرَضاً—sick from grief, nearing destruction أَوْ تَكُونَ مِنَ الْهالِكِينَ ** or become one of the perishing ones (الميّتين).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The great weepers (البكّاؤن) are five... until he said: As for Jacob, he wept over Joseph until his sight departed, until it was said to him: **تَاللهِ تَفْتَؤُا ** (the rest of) the Ayah.

(٨٦) قالَ إِنَّما أَشْكُوا بَثِّي وَحُزْنِي ** (86) He said, 'I only complain of my distress and grief My anxiety (همّي) which I am unable to bear patiently إِلَى اللهِ ** to Allah, not to anyone other than Him, so leave me and my complaint. وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللهِ ** and I know from Allah from His action (صنعه) and His mercy (رحمته) ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** what you do not know.' and my good expectation (حسن ظنّي) of Him is that He will bring me relief (بالفرج) from where I do not anticipate. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Benjamin was taken from Jacob (يعقوب), he called out: "O Lord, will You not have mercy on me? You have taken my sight and You have taken my son!" So Allah Almighty revealed to him: "Had I caused them both to die, I would have resurrected them for you until I reunited you with them. But remember the sheep (الشاة) which you slaughtered and roasted and ate, while so-and-so and so-and-so were beside you fasting, and you did not give them anything from it."

(87) يا بَنِيَّ اذْهَبُوا فَتَحَسَّسُوا مِنْ يُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ (87) 'O my sons! Go and look for Joseph and his brother, Investigate their condition and seek news of them. وَلا تَيْأَسُوا مِنْ رَوْحِ اللهِ and do not despair of Allah’s comfort. Do not despair of His relief (فرجه), His alleviation (تنفيسه), and His mercy. إِنَّهُ لا يَيْأَسُ مِنْ رَوْحِ اللهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْكافِرُونَ Indeed no one despairs of Allah’s comfort except the faithless lot.' Because the believer is assured of good from Allah, which he hopes for during affliction and for which he thanks Him during ease (الرّخاءِ).

In al-Kafi, al-Ilal (العلل), al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), and al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether Ya‘qub, when He said to His sons: اذْهَبُوا فَتَحَسَّسُوا مِنْ يُوسُفَ (Go and look for Joseph), knew that he was alive, despite having been separated from him for twenty years and having lost His sight due to grief. He (peace be upon him) said: "Yes, He knew that he was alive." He was asked: "And how did He know?" He (peace be upon him) said: "He supplicated at dawn (السّحر) that the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) descend upon Him. So, Turabal (ترابال), who is the Angel of Death, descended upon Him. Turabal said to Him: 'What is your need, O Ya‘qub?' He said: 'Inform me about the souls: do you seize them collectively or separately?' He replied: 'Rather, separately, soul by soul.' He asked: 'Did the soul of Yusuf pass by you?' He replied: 'No.' It was then that He knew he was alive, so He said to His sons: اذْهَبُوا فَتَحَسَّسُوا مِنْ يُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ (Go and look for Joseph and his brother)."

And in al-Ikmal (الإِكمال), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): a similar account, in brief.

And in al-Khara'ij (الخرايج), narrated from him (peace be upon him): An Arab (اعرابيّاً) bought food from Yusuf, who said to him: "When you pass by such-and-such a valley, call out: 'O Ya‘qub!' A shaykh will come out to you. Tell him: 'I saw a man in Egypt who sends you greetings (يقرؤك السلام) and says that your trust (وديعتك) with Allah is preserved and will not be lost.'" When the Arab conveyed this to Him, Ya‘qub fell down unconscious (مغشيّاً عليه). When He recovered, He asked: "Do you have any need?" He replied: "I have a paternal cousin's daughter who is my wife, and she has not borne children." So, He supplicated for him, and he was granted four pregnancies (أربعة أبطن) from her, with two children in each pregnancy.

And in al-Ikmal (الإِكمال), a similar account, but more detailed, and it states: "A great, handsome, and comely man will come out to you." And it said at the end of it: Ya‘qub knew that Yusuf was alive and had not died, and that Allah would reveal him to Him after his absence. He used to say to His sons: أَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ (I know from Allah what you do not know). His sons, His family, and His relatives used to criticize Him (يفنّدونه) for mentioning Yusuf.

(88) فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ after they returned to Egypt قالُوا يا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنا وَأَهْلَنَا الضُّرُّ hardship (الشدة) وَجِئْنا بِبِضاعَةٍ مُزْجاةٍ poor/worthless (رديّة). When they entered into his presence, they said, 'O noble sir, distress has afflicted us and our family, and we have brought a paltry commodity. ** Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): It was al-muql (المقل), and their land was the land of al-muql (المقل), and that is the commodity (البضاعة). فَأَوْفِ لَنَا الْكَيْلَ وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنا and favor us by overlooking [the deficiency] and increase us beyond our due right, or [be charitable] with our brother Benjamin, as will come إِنَّ اللهَ يَجْزِي الْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ He rewards them for their charities with something better than them. so give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards the charitable.'** Yusuf felt pity for them and could not restrain himself from making himself known to them.

(٨٩) **قالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ جاهِلُونَ ** (89) He said, 'Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother, when you were ignorant?'

He said this out of compassion (شفقة) and advice (نصحاً) when He saw their helplessness (عجزهم) and humility (تمسكنهم), not as a reproach (معاتبة) or blame (تثريباً), preferring the right of Allah over His own right in that station where the afflicted one breathes out (his pain). And perhaps their action toward his brother (Benjamin) was separating him from Joseph; and it was said: and humiliating him until he was unable to speak to them except with helplessness (عجز) and abasement (ذلّة).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every sin that the servant commits, even if he is knowledgeable (عالماً), he is ignorant (جاهل) when he risks his soul (نفسه) by disobeying his Lord. Thus, Allah, the Glorified, related the saying of Joseph to his brothers: هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ جاهِلُونَ ** (Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother, when you were ignorant?). So, He attributed ignorance (الجهل) to them because they risked their souls (بأنفسهم) in disobedience to Allah.

(٩٠) قالُوا (١) أَإِنَّكَ لَأَنْتَ يُوسُفُ ** (90) They said, 'Are you indeed Joseph?' This is an interrogative for confirmation (استفهام تقرير), and it was recited as an affirmative (على الإِيجاب). He said: "I am Joseph, and this is my brother from my father and my mother." He mentioned this as an introduction (تعريفاً) of himself and an exaltation (تفخيماً) of his status. قَدْ مَنَّ اللهُ عَلَيْنا ** 'Allah has indeed been gracious to us. Meaning, with safety (بالسلامة) and honor (والكرامَة). إِنَّهُ مَنْ يَتَّقِ ** Indeed, whoever is Godwary Meaning, whoever fears Allah (من يتّق الله). وَيَصْبِرْ ** and is patient— Over afflictions (على البليّات) and from acts of disobedience (وعن المعاصي). فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ. Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.'

(٩١) قالُوا تَاللهِ لَقَدْ آثَرَكَ اللهُ عَلَيْنا ** (91) They said, 'By Allah, Allah has indeed preferred you to us, He chose you over us by the beauty of the form and the perfection of the conduct. وَإِنْ كُنَّا لَخاطِئِينَ ** and we have indeed been sinners.' And our affair and our condition is that we were indeed sinners in what we did to you. Undoubtedly, Allah has honored you and humiliated us. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They said: "So do not disgrace us and do not punish us today, and forgive us."

(92) قَالَ لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ يَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ (He said, 'There shall be no reproach for you today. May Allah forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful.') قالَ لا تَثْرِيبَ means: No fault (لا عيب), nor shaming (ولا تعيير), nor reprimand (ولا تأنيب). عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ regarding what you have done (فيما فعلتم). يَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ.

In al-Majma' (of al-Bayan), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a long hadith: That Jacob wrote to Joseph: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. To the Aziz of Egypt, the manifestor of justice, and the fulfiller of the measure (موفي الكيل), from Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham, the intimate friend (خليل) of the All-beneficent, the companion of Nimrod who gathered the fire for him to burn him with it, but Allah made it cool and safe for him and saved him from it. I inform you, O Aziz, that we are a household (أهل بيت) to whom affliction has always been swift from Allah, so that He may test us in times of ease (السّرّاءِ) and hardship (الضّرّاء). Indeed, calamities have followed one another upon me for twenty years. The first of them was that I had a son whom I named Joseph, and he was my joy among my children, the delight of my eye, and the fruit of my heart. His brothers, who were not from his mother, asked me to send him with them to graze and play. So, I sent him with them in the morning, and they came back to me in the evening weeping, bringing his shirt with false blood, claiming that the wolf had eaten him. My grief for his loss intensified, and my weeping over his separation increased until my eyes turned white from sorrow. And he had a brother whom I admired and who was a comfort to me, and whenever I remembered Joseph, I would embrace him to my chest. His brothers mentioned that you asked them about him and commanded them to bring him to you, and if they did not bring him to you, you would withhold the provision (الميرة) from them. So, I sent him with them so that they might procure wheat for us. They returned to me, and he was not with them, and they mentioned that he had stolen the King's measure (مكيال). We are a household (أهل بيت) who do not steal. You have detained him from me and afflicted me with grief over him. My sorrow over his separation has intensified until my back has become bent because of it, and my calamity concerning him has become great, along with the calamities that have followed upon me. So, grant me favor by releasing him and freeing him from your imprisonment, and make the wheat good for us, and be lenient with us in the price, and fulfill the measure for us, and hasten the release (سراح) (1) of the family of Abraham (آل إبراهيم).

He said: So they proceeded with his letter until they entered upon Joseph in the King's palace, and قَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا الضُّرُّ (They said, 'O Aziz! Distress has afflicted us and our family')—to the end of the Ayah—وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنَا (and be charitable to us) by [releasing] our brother Benjamin. "And this is the letter of our father Jacob, which he sent to you concerning his matter, asking you to release him. So, grant us favor with him." Joseph took Jacob's letter, kissed it, placed it upon his eyes, wept, and sobbed (وانتحبَ) (2) until his tears soaked the shirt he was wearing. Then he turned to them and قَالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ مَا فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ (He said, 'Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother') previously (من قبل).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith of his, he said: Jacob's grief intensified until his back became bent (تقوس) (3), and the world turned away from him and his children until they were in severe need and their provision (ميرتهم) was exhausted. It was then that Jacob said to his children: اذْهَبُوا فَتَحَسَّسُوا (Go and look for news)—the Ayah. So, a group (نفر) of them departed, and he sent them with a small amount of merchandise (بضاعة يسيرة), and he wrote a letter with them to the Aziz of Egypt, seeking to incline him toward himself and his children. He instructed his children to present his letter before the merchandise. So, he wrote, mentioning the description of the letter similar to what was mentioned in al-Majma' up to His saying: "and hasten the release of the family of Abraham (آل إبراهيم)." (He mentioned the family of Jacob (آل يعقوب) instead of the family of Abraham (آل إبراهيم)). Then he said: When Jacob's children departed from him toward Egypt with his letter, Gabriel descended upon Jacob and said to him: "O Jacob, your Lord says to you: 'Who afflicted you with the calamities about which you wrote to the Aziz of Egypt?'" Jacob said: "You afflicted me with them, as a punishment (عقوبة) from You and a discipline (أدباً) for me." Allah said: "Was anyone besides Me able to avert them from you?" Jacob said: "O Allah, no." He said: "Then why were you not ashamed before Me when you complained of your calamities to someone other than Me, and did not seek My help and complain of what afflicted you to Me?" Jacob said: "I seek Your forgiveness, O my God, and I repent to You, and I complain of my distress (بثّي) and my sorrow to You." Allah Almighty said: "I have reached the limit with you, O Jacob, and with your sinning children, in My discipline (أدبي). If you, O Jacob, had complained of your calamities to Me when they descended upon you, and sought forgiveness and repented to Me for your sin, I would have averted them from you even after I had decreed them upon you. But Satan made you forget My remembrance, so you fell into despair of My mercy. I am Allah, the Generous (الجواد), the Noble (الكريم). I love My servants who seek forgiveness, who repent, and who desire what is with Me. O Jacob, I am returning Joseph and his brother to you, and restoring to you what has departed from your wealth, your flesh, and your blood, and restoring your sight to you, and straightening your back for you. Be content in soul and delighted in eye. What I did to you was merely discipline (أدباً) from Me to you, so accept My discipline." He said: Jacob's children proceeded with his letter, to the end of what was mentioned in al-Majma', except that he said: "And he had a brother from his maternal aunt, and I admired him." Then he mentioned the description of the letter in another, shorter narration, and said at the end of it: When Joseph (peace be upon him) was brought the letter, he opened it and read it, and he cried out. Then he stood up and entered his house, read it, and wept. Then he washed his face and went out to his brothers. Then he returned, read it, cried out, and wept. Then he stood up and entered his house, read it, and wept. Then he washed his face and returned to his brothers, and قَالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ مَا فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ جَاهِلُونَ (He said, 'Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant?') And he gave them his shirt, which was the shirt of Abraham, and Jacob was in al-Ramlah.

(1) It is said that when Joseph said to them هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ (Do you know)—the Ayah—he smiled. When they saw his front teeth, which were like strung pearls, they likened him to Joseph and said to him: "Indeed, you are Joseph." And it is said that they recognized him when he removed the crown from his head. (م ن).

(1) You say سَرَّحْتُ فُلَانًا to such-and-such a place when you send him. And تَسْرِيحُ الْمَرْأَةِ is divorcing her. The noun (الاسم) is السَّرَاح (al-sarāḥ), like التَّبْلِيغ (al-tablīgh) and الْبَلَاغ (al-balāgh). And تَسْرِيحُ الشَّعْرِ is letting it down and untying it before combing.

(2) النَّحْب (al-naḥb) is the most intense weeping, like النَّحِيب (al-naḥīb). The verb forms are نَحَبَ (like مَنَعَ) and انْتَحَبَ.

(3) قَوَّسَ تَقْوِيسًا means he bent, like تَقَوَّسَ.

(٩٣) اذْهَبُوا بِقَمِيصِي هذا فَأَلْقُوهُ عَلى وَجْهِ أَبِي يَأْتِ بَصِيراً وَأْتُونِي (93) 'Take this shirt of mine and cast it on my father's face, and he will regain his sight, and bring me' (you and my father) with all your family (بِأَهْلِكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ). (٩٤) وَلَمَّا فَصَلَتِ الْعِيرُ (94) When the caravan set out, from Egypt and departed from its inhabited area (عمرانها) قالَ أَبُوهُمْ their father said to those who were present with him (لمن حضره) إِنِّي لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ لَوْ لا أَنْ تُفَنِّدُونِ 'I sense the scent of Joseph, were it not that you would call me a dotard.' meaning: you would attribute me to الفَنَد (al-fanad), which is a deficiency of intellect (نقصان عقل) that occurs due to old age (الهَرَم). And the apodosis (جواب) of لو لا (law lā) is omitted (محذوف), and its implied meaning (تقديره) is: you would have believed me (لصدّقتموني).

(٩٥) قالُوا تَاللهِ إِنَّكَ لَفِي ضَلالِكَ الْقَدِيمِ ** (95) They said, 'By Allah, you are indeed in your age-old error.' [Meaning:] in your deviation from the truth since olden times, due to your excessiveness in the love of Yusuf, your frequent mention of him, and your expectation of meeting him. (٩٦) فَلَمَّا أَنْ جاءَ الْبَشِيرُ ** (96) So when the bearer of good news came, In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was his son, Yahudha (يهودا). أَلْقاهُ عَلى وَجْهِهِ ** he cast the shirt upon his face فَارْتَدَّ بَصِيراً ** and he regained his sight. He returned seeing, due to the strength that revived within him. قالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** He said, 'Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know?' [Meaning:] concerning the life of Yusuf and the sending down of relief (الفرج) from Allah. And it is possible that إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ ** (I know) is a new sentence (مستأنفاً) and the object of the saying (المقول) is omitted, indicated by the preceding speech. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Aziz of Egypt wrote to Ya'qub: "To proceed: This is your son Yusuf, whom I bought بِثَمَنٍ بَخْسٍ دَراهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ ** (for a paltry price, a few dirhams) and took him as a slave. And this is your son Binyamin, who stole, so I took him as a slave." He (the Imam) said: Nothing came upon Ya'qub that was harder for him than that letter. So he said to the messenger: "Stay where you are until I answer him." So he wrote to him: "To proceed: I have understood your letter, [stating] that you took my son for a paltry price and took him as a slave, and that you took my son Binyamin, who stole, and took him as a slave. Indeed, we are a household that does not steal, but we are a household that is tested. Our father Ibrahim was tested by the fire, and Allah protected him. Our father Ishaq was tested by the sacrifice, and Allah protected him. And I have been tested by the loss of my sight and the loss of my two sons, and عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَأْتِيَنِي بِهِمْ جَمِيعاً ** (Maybe Allah will bring them all to me)." He said: When the messenger departed from him, he raised his hand to the sky, then said: "O Good Companion (يا حَسَنَ الصُّحبة), O Generous Helper (يا كريمَ المعونة), O All Good (يا خيراً كُلّه), bring me comfort (روحٍ) and relief (فرج) from Yourself." He said: Then Jibril descended upon him and said to Ya'qub: "Shall I not teach you supplications (دعوات) by which Allah will restore your sight to you and restore your two sons to you?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Say: O He Whom no one knows how He is, where He is, and His power, except Him! O He Who sealed the air with the sky, and compressed the earth upon the water, and chose for Himself the most beautiful Names (أَحْسَنَ الأسماء), bring me comfort (روحٍ) from You and relief (فرج) from You." He said: The column of dawn had not yet broken when the shirt was brought to him and cast upon his face, and Allah restored his sight to him and restored his children to him. And al-Qummi narrated this hadith in a more detailed manner than this, and mentioned in the letter of the Aziz, instead of "who stole": "I found my goods with him." And he mentioned in Ya'qub's reply: his being tested (ابتلاءه) with his two sons, similar to his letter mentioned previously. And he said in it [the narrative]: "He had a brother from his mother whom I used to find comfort in, and he went out with his brothers..." until he said: "...and I have detained him, and I ask you by the God of Ibrahim, Ishaq, and Ya'qub, that you grant him to me as a favor (إلّا مننت عليّ به) and draw near to Allah by returning him to me." He said: When the letter reached Yusuf, he took it, placed it on his face, kissed it, and wept intensely. Then he looked at his brothers and said to them: هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ ** (Do you know what you did to Yusuf) and the following verses. He said: Then the messenger departed, [ending] the hadith. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said: اذْهَبُوا بِقَمِيصِي هذا ** (Go with this shirt of mine) which the tears of my eyes have moistened فَأَلْقُوهُ عَلى وَجْهِ أَبِي يَأْتِ بَصِيراً ** (and cast it on my father's face, he will regain his sight) if he smells my scent وَأْتُونِي بِأَهْلِكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** (and bring me all your family). And he sent them back to Ya'qub on that very day and equipped them with everything they needed. So لَمَّا فَصَلَتِ ** (When their caravan departed) from Egypt, Ya'qub perceived the scent of Yusuf, and he said to those of his children who were present: إِنِّي لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ لَوْ لا أَنْ تُفَنِّدُونِ ** (Indeed, I sense the scent of Yusuf, unless you think me senile). He said: And his children approached, urging the journey with the shirt, joyful and delighted by what they had seen of Yusuf's condition, the dominion that Allah had granted him, and the honor they had attained under Yusuf's authority. Their journey from Egypt to Ya'qub took nine days. فَلَمَّا أَنْ جاءَ الْبَشِيرُ أَلْقاهُ ** (So when the bearer of good news came, he cast it)—the shirt—عَلى وَجْهِهِ فَارْتَدَّ بَصِيراً ** (on his face, and he regained his sight). And he asked them: "What happened to the son of Yamil?" They said: "We left him with his brother, safe and sound (صالحاً)." He said: Ya'qub then praised Allah and prostrated to his Lord a prostration of gratitude (سجدة الشكر). His sight returned to him, and his back straightened. He said to his children: "Move to Yusuf this very day, all of you." So they went to Yusuf, and with them were Ya'qub and Yusuf's maternal aunt, Yamil. They urged the journey with joy and delight, traveling for nine days to Egypt. And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Ya'qub perceived the scent of Ibrahim's shirt when the caravan departed from Egypt, while he was in Palestine. In al-Kafi, al-Ikmal, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Do you know what Yusuf's shirt was?" It was said: "No." He said: "When the fire was kindled for Ibrahim, Jibril descended to him with the shirt." And al-Qummi [added]: "With a garment from the garments of Paradise (الجنة), and he clothed him with it, so neither heat nor cold harmed him with it. When death approached him, he placed it in his amulet (تميمته) and hung it upon Ishaq, and Ishaq hung it upon Ya'qub. When Yusuf was born, he hung it upon him, and it was on his arm until what happened to him happened. When Yusuf took it out of the amulet in Egypt, Ya'qub perceived its scent, and that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, relating from him: إِنِّي لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ لَوْ لا أَنْ تُفَنِّدُونِ ** (Indeed, I sense the scent of Yusuf, unless you think me senile). And that is the shirt that was sent down from Paradise (الجنّة)." It was asked: "May I be made your ransom, to whom did this shirt pass?" He said: "To its people. Then it will be with our Qa'im (قائمنا) when he emerges." Then he said: "Every Prophet who inherited knowledge or anything else, it has reached Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." And al-Qummi added: "Ya'qub was in Palestine, and the caravan departed from Egypt, and Ya'qub perceived its scent, and it was from that shirt which was sent down from Paradise (الجنّة), and we are its inheritors." And al-Ayyashi, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): Ya'qub perceived the scent of Yusuf's shirt from a distance of ten nights' journey. Ya'qub was in Jerusalem (بيت المقدس) and Yusuf was in Egypt. It was the shirt that descended upon Ibrahim from Paradise (الجنّة) in a silver tube (قصبة من فضّة). When it was worn, it was wide and large. When they departed, and Ya'qub was in al-Ramlah (الرّملة) and Yusuf was in Egypt, Ya'qub said: إِنِّي لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ (Indeed, I sense the scent of Yusuf), meaning the scent of Paradise (الجنّة), when they departed with the shirt, because it was from Paradise (الجنّة). I say: This means that it was from the world of al-Malakut (الملكوت) and the inner reality (الباطن), which had manifested into the world of al-Mulk (الملك) and the outer reality (الظاهر), and thus became perceptible.

(٩٧) قالُوا يا أَبانَا اسْتَغْفِرْ لَنا ذُنُوبَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا خاطِئِينَ. (97) They said, 'Father, plead with Allah to forgive us our sins. We have indeed been sinners.' (٩٨) قالَ سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّي إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ. (98) He said, 'I shall soon plead with my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.'

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The best time for you to supplicate to Allah is during the pre-dawn hours (الأسحار)." Then He recited this verse regarding Jacob's (يعقوب) saying: سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّي ** (I shall soon plead with my Lord to forgive you), and He said: "He delayed them until the pre-dawn hour (السّحر)."

And in al-Faqih, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He delayed it until the pre-dawn hour (السّحر) of the night of Friday (ليلة الجمعة).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He delayed them until the pre-dawn hour (السحر) and said: "O Lord, their sin is only concerning what is between me and them." So Allah revealed: "I have indeed forgiven them."

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about Jacob (يعقوب), that when his sons said to him: يا أَبانَا اسْتَغْفِرْ لَنا ذُنُوبَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا خاطِئِينَ قالَ سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّي ** (Father, plead with Allah to forgive us our sins. We have indeed been sinners. He said, 'I shall soon plead with my Lord to forgive you'), he delayed seeking forgiveness for them. Whereas Joseph (يوسف), when they said to him: تَاللهِ لَقَدْ آثَرَكَ اللهُ عَلَيْنا وَإِنْ كُنَّا لَخاطِئِينَ قالَ لا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ يَغْفِرُ اللهُ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ ** (By Allah, Allah has indeed preferred you over us, and we were certainly sinners. He said, 'There shall be no reproach upon you today. May Allah forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful'), He (peace be upon him) said: "That is because the heart of the youth (الشّابّ) is softer than the heart of the elder (الشيخ). The offense (جناية) of Jacob's (يعقوب) sons was against Joseph (يوسف), and their offense against Jacob was only due to their offense against Joseph. So Joseph hastened to pardon his own right (حقّه), while Jacob delayed the pardon, because his pardon was concerning the right of another. Thus, he delayed them until the pre-dawn hour (السّحر) of the night of Friday (ليلة الجمعة)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether Jacob's (يعقوب) children were prophets (أنبياء). He said: "No, but they were tribes (أسباطاً), the children of prophets. They did not depart the world except as fortunate ones (سعداء); they repented and remembered what they had done. However, the two elders (الشيخين) departed the world without repenting and without remembering what they had done to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). So upon them is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all mankind."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether Joseph's (يوسف) brothers were prophets (أنبياء). He said: "No, but they were righteous (بررة), pious (أتقياء). How could they be [prophets] when they said to their father Jacob (يعقوب): تَاللهِ إِنَّكَ لَفِي ضَلالِكَ الْقَدِيمِ. (By Allah, you are indeed in your age-old error.)"

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the state of Jacob's (يعقوب) sons: "Did they leave the faith (الإيمان)?" He said: "Yes." I said: "Then what do you say about Adam (آدم)?" He said: "Leave Adam (peace be upon him)."

(٩٩) فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلى يُوسُفَ آوى إِلَيْهِ أَبَوَيْهِ ** (99) Then, when they entered into Joseph's presence, he sheltered his parents He embraced his father and his mother, Rachel (راحيل), as was narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) at the beginning of the Surah concerning the interpretation of the dream; or [it means] his father and his maternal aunt, Yamīl (ياميل), based on what preceded in the narration of al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ): that she was the one who went with them to Egypt. And based on what will come in his narration: that he raised his father and his maternal aunt onto the royal throne. If this narration is authentic (صحّت), then perhaps she was treated as a mother, just as the paternal uncle was treated as a father in His saying: وَإِلهَ آبائِكَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ ** (and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael). And because it was narrated: that she raised him after his mother, and the foster mother (الرّابّة) is called a mother. **وَقالَ ادْخُلُوا مِصْرَ إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ آمِنِينَ ** (and said, 'Enter Egypt, God willing, in safety.') Meaning (1) if God wills, you shall enter it safely. They only entered into his presence before entering Egypt because Joseph met them and settled them in a house or a tent there, so they entered his presence and he embraced his parents.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Elder Ya‘qub (الشيخ يعقوب) came to Joseph, the glory of kingship (عزّ الملك) entered him, so he did not dismount for him. Jibrā’īl (Gabriel) descended upon him and said: "O Joseph, extend your palm." A brilliant light emerged from it and ascended into the atmosphere of the sky. Joseph said: "O Jibrā’īl, what is this light that emerged from my palm?" He said: "The prophethood (النّبوّة) has been removed from your progeny as a punishment for your failure to dismount for the Elder Ya‘qub, so there shall be no prophet among your descendants."

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Joseph met Ya‘qub, Ya‘qub dismounted for him, but Joseph did not dismount for him. He had not yet separated from the embrace when Jibrā’īl came to him and said: "O Joseph, the Truthful One (الصديق) dismounted for you, yet you did not dismount for him. Extend your hand." And he mentioned the same as what is in al-Kafi. And in another narration: He intended to dismount for Ya‘qub, then he looked at the kingship (الملك) he was in, and he did not do it. (The rest of the Hadith).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): When Ya‘qub, his family, and his children arrived in Egypt, Joseph sat upon his throne and placed the crown of kingship upon his head, desiring that his father see him in that state. When his father entered his presence, he did not stand up for him, so they all fell down prostrating. Then he narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): Jibrā’īl removed the light of prophethood (نور النبوّة) from between his fingers and erased it from his loins (صلبه), placing it in the progeny of his brother Levi (لاوي) because he had forbidden his brothers from killing him, and because he said: فَلَنْ أَبْرَحَ الْأَرْضَ (I will not leave this land) (the Ayah). He said: So Allah thanked Him for that, and the prophets of the Children of Israel were from his progeny, and Moses (موسى) was from his progeny. He is Moses son of ‘Imrān son of Yishar (يصهر) son of Qāhith (واهث) son of Levi (لاوي) son of Ya‘qub.

footnote (١) والاستثناء يعود الى الأمن وإنّما قال **آمِنِينَ **لأنّهم كانوا فيما خلا يخافون ملوك مصر ولا يدخلونها إلا بجوازهم ، قال وَهَبْ أنهم دخلوا مصر وهم ثلاث وسبعون إنساناً وخرجوا مع موسى وهم ستمائة الف وخمسمائة وبضع وسبعون رجلاً مجمع البيان. (1) The exception (الاستثناء) refers back to the safety (الأمن). He only said in safety because previously they used to fear the kings of Egypt and would not enter it except with their permission. Wahb (وَهَبْ) said: They entered Egypt while they were seventy-three people, and they departed with Moses while they were six hundred thousand, five hundred, and seventy-odd men. (Majma‘ al-Bayan).

(١٠٠) وَرَفَعَ أَبَوَيْهِ وَخَرُّوا لَهُ سُجَّداً. (100) And he seated his parents on the throne, and they fell down before him, prostrating.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Throne (العرش) was the couch (السرير), and that prostration of theirs was worship of Allah. وَقالَ يا أَبَتِ هذا تَأْوِيلُ رُؤْيايَ مِنْ قَبْلُ قَدْ جَعَلَها رَبِّي حَقًّا (And he said, 'Father! This is the interpretation of my dream of long ago. My Lord has made it come true.') He (Joseph) had seen it in the days of childhood.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked how many of his children Jacob entered upon Joseph with. He said: "With eleven sons of his." He was asked: "Were they prophets (أسباط)?" He said: "Yes."

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When they entered upon Joseph in the royal palace, he embraced his father and wept, and he seated him and seated his maternal aunt upon the royal couch. Then he entered his dwelling, applied oil (ادّهن), applied kohl (اكتحل), and wore the garments of glory and kingship. Then he went out to them, and when they saw him, they prostrated to him out of reverence and gratitude to Allah. It was then that he said: يا أَبَتِ هذا تَأْوِيلُ رُؤْيايَ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (‘Father! This is the interpretation of my dream of long ago.') He (the Imam) said: Joseph had not applied oil, nor kohl, nor perfume, nor laughed, nor touched women during those twenty years, until Allah reunited him with Jacob and brought him together with Jacob and his brothers. In al-Majma', a similar narration is reported from him (peace be upon him).
