I say: Perhaps the intent behind the negation of his touching women is the absence of touching them for pleasure (الالتذاذ) and desire (الشهوة). This does not contradict what preceded, that he had a son playing with a pomegranate before him when his brother disputed with him concerning his (Joseph's) brother. Perhaps he only touched them to make the earth heavy with the glorification (تسبيح) of the child, as passed in the excuse of his brother in a similar instance.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When they entered upon him, they prostrated in gratitude to Allah alone when they looked at him, and that prostration was for Allah.

And from Imam al-Hadi (peace be upon him): He was asked about the prostration of Jacob and his children to Joseph (peace be upon him), while they were prophets. He said: "As for the prostration of Jacob and his children, it was not for Joseph. Rather, it was from Jacob and his children as obedience to Allah and a greeting (تحيّة) to Joseph, just as the prostration of the angels to Adam was only obedience to Allah and a greeting to Adam. So Jacob and his children prostrated, and Joseph prostrated with them, in gratitude to Allah for the reunion of their family. Do you not see that he says in his gratitude at that time: **رَبِّ قَدْ آتَيْتَنِي مِنَ الْمُلْكِ ** (My Lord! You have granted me a share of the kingdom,) (the rest of) the Ayah."

In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited: "And they fell down before Allah, prostrating." وَقَدْ أَحْسَنَ بِي إِذْ أَخْرَجَنِي مِنَ السِّجْنِ ** (And He has been good to me when He brought me out of the prison,) Perhaps he did not mention the pit (الجبّ) so that it would not be a reproach (تثريباً) against them. وَجاءَ بِكُمْ مِنَ الْبَدْوِ ** (and brought you from the desert) —from the wilderness (البادية)—because they were owners of livestock (أصحاب المواشي) and people of the desert (أهل البدو), moving between waters and pastures (المناجع) (1). مِنْ بَعْدِ أَنْ نَزَغَ الشَّيْطانُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ إِخْوَتِي ** (after Satan had incited strife between me and my brothers.) He caused corruption (أفسد) between us and instigated conflict (حرّش). إِنَّ رَبِّي لَطِيفٌ لِما يَشاءُ ** (Indeed my Lord is subtle in what He wills.) In the management (تدبير) of His servants, He makes difficulty easy for them and makes it gentle. إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَلِيمُ ** (Indeed He is the All-knowing,) of the aspects of benefits (المصالح) and management (التدابير), الْحَكِيمُ ** (the All-wise,) Who does everything at its proper time and in the manner required by His wisdom.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Hadi (peace be upon him): Jacob said to his son: "Tell me what your brothers did to you when they took you away from me." He said: "O Father, excuse me from that." He said: "Then tell me some of it." He said: "When they brought me near the pit (الجبّ), they said: 'Take off the shirt.' I said to them: 'O my brothers, fear Allah and do not strip me naked.' So they drew a knife (السكّين) against me and said: 'If you do not take it off, we will slaughter you.' So I took off the shirt, and they threw me into the pit naked." He (the narrator) said: Jacob gasped a gasp (شهقة) and fainted. When he recovered, he said: "O my son, tell me more." He said: "O Father, I ask you by the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, that you excuse me." So he excused him.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that which is similar in meaning.

And it is narrated in al-Majma': Joseph (peace be upon him) said to Jacob: "Do not ask me about the deed (صنيع) of my brothers, but ask about the deed of Allah concerning me."

(1) In the narration of Ali (peace be upon him): It—meaning the world (الدّنيا)—is a temporary dwelling (منزل قلعة) and not a permanent abode (دار نجعة). His saying: "temporary dwelling (منزل قلعة)" (قلعة with a dammah on the qaf) means it is unsuitable for permanent settlement (الاستيطان). And al-naj'ah (النجعة with a dammah on the nun) also means seeking pasture (الكلاء). The summary of this is that it is not a dwelling of rest and pleasant life. Al-Intija' (الانتجاع) is the seeking of benevolence (الإحسان). From this is the phrase: "I sought benevolence from so-and-so (انتجعت فلاناً)" when you came to him seeking his favor (معروفه). And al-Intija' is the seeking of vegetation (النّبات), fodder (العلف), and water. (م.)

(101) رَبِّ قَدْ آتَيْتَنِي مِنَ الْمُلْكِ ** (My Lord! You have granted me a share of the kingdom,) Some of the kingdom, which is the kingdom of Egypt. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith where he mentions Yusuf: "It was part of his affair that he chose the kingdom [the kingdom of Egypt, apparently] and what surrounded it up to Yemen." In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, did not send prophets as kings on earth except four..." until he said: "As for Yusuf, he ruled Egypt and its deserts, and did not go beyond it to other lands." وَعَلَّمْتَنِي مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحادِيثِ ** (and taught me the interpretation of dreams.) Some of their interpretation. فاطِرَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (Creator of the heavens and the earth!) Their Originator (مبدعهما). أَنْتَ وَلِيِّي ** (You are my Guardian) My Helper (ناصري) and the Manager of my affair (متوليّ أمري). فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ ** (in this world and the Hereafter.) You manage me with favor in both of them, and You connect the fleeting kingdom (الملك الفاني) with the everlasting Kingdom (الملك الباقي). تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِماً وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ ** (Take my soul as a Muslim, and unite me with the righteous.') In rank (الرتبة) and honor (الكرامة).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather, from the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Ya'qub ibn Ishaq lived one hundred and forty years, and Yusuf ibn Ya'qub lived one hundred and twenty years." In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Yusuf entered the prison when he was twelve years old, remained there for eighteen years, and lived eighty years after his release. That makes one hundred and ten years." And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked how long Ya'qub lived with Yusuf in Egypt. He said: "He lived two years (حولين)." It was asked: "Who was the Proof (الحجّة) of Allah on earth, Ya'qub or Yusuf?" He said: "It was Ya'qub, and the kingship was for Yusuf. When Ya'qub died, Yusuf carried him in a coffin to the land of Sham (الشام) and buried him in Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس). Yusuf (peace be upon him) was the Proof (الحجّة) after Ya'qub." It was asked: "Was Yusuf a Messenger (رسولاً) and a Prophet (نبيّاً)?" He said: "Yes. Do you not hear His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ يُوسُفُ مِنْ قَبْلُ بِالْبَيِّناتِ. (And certainly Joseph came to you before with manifest proofs.)" And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) something close to it. In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah revealed to Musa ibn 'Imran (موسى بن عِمْرانَ) to take the bones of Yusuf out of Egypt. So he extracted them from the bank of the Nile (النّيل). They were in a marble box. He carried them to Sham (الشام). That is why the People of the Book carry their dead to Sham. And this is Yusuf ibn Ya'qub, and Allah did not mention any other Yusuf in the Quran besides him." In al-'Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Zulaykha sought permission to enter upon Yusuf. It was said to her: 'We dislike presenting you to him because of what you did to him.' She said: 'I do not fear one who fears Allah.' When she entered, he said to her: 'O Zulaykha, why do I see your color changed?' She said: 'Praise be to Allah Who made kings slaves due to their disobedience, and made slaves kings due to their obedience.' He said to her: 'What led you to do what you did?' She said: 'The beauty of your face, O Yusuf.' He said: 'What if you saw a Prophet called Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم who will appear at the end of time, more beautiful in face than me, better in character than me, and more generous in hand than me?' She said: 'You have spoken the truth.' He asked: 'How did you know that I spoke the truth?' She said: 'Because when you mentioned Him, His love fell into my heart.' So Allah revealed to Yusuf that she had spoken the truth, and 'I have loved her because of her love for Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم.' Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded him to marry her." And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Hadi (peace be upon him): "When al-'Aziz died during the years of drought, the wife of al-'Aziz became poor and needy, to the point that she begged. They said to her: 'If only you would sit for al-'Aziz (and Yusuf was called al-'Aziz, and every king they had was called by this name).' She said: 'I am ashamed before him.' They persisted with her until she sat for him. Yusuf approached in his procession. She stood up to him and said: 'Glory be to Him Who made kings slaves through disobedience, and made slaves kings through obedience.' Yusuf said to her: 'Are you [that woman]?' She said: 'Yes,' and her name was Zulaykha. He said to her: 'Do you have any desire?' She said: 'Leave me alone after I have grown old. Are you mocking me?' He said: 'No.' She said: 'Yes.' So he commanded that she be moved to his house, and she was an old woman. He said to her: 'Did you not do such-and-such to me?' She said: 'O Prophet of Allah, do not blame me, for I was afflicted by three things by which no one else was afflicted.' He asked: 'And what are they?' She said: 'I was afflicted by love for you, and Allah did not create an equal to you in this world. And I was afflicted by the fact that there was no woman in Egypt more beautiful or wealthier than me, yet it was taken away from me. And I was afflicted by an impotent husband (زوج عنين) (1).' Yusuf said to her: 'What do you desire?' She said: 'That you ask Allah to restore my youth to me.' So he asked Allah, and He restored her youth to her, and he married her while she was a virgin."

(١٠٢) ذلِكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ ** (102) (That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you) O Muhammad, وَما كُنْتَ لَدَيْهِمْ ** (and you were not with them) with the brothers of Joseph إِذْ أَجْمَعُوا أَمْرَهُمْ ** (when they resolved upon their plan) They resolved upon what they intended (عزموا على ما همّوا به), وَهُمْ يَمْكُرُونَ ** (while they were plotting) That was not known except through revelation (بالوحي).

(103) وَما أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتَ regarding their belief, and you exerted yourself in manifesting the signs upon them بِمُؤْمِنِينَ due to their obstinacy (عناد) and their determination (تصميم) upon disbelief (الكفر). وَما أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ (١٠٣) وَلَوْ حَرَصْتَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (103) Yet most people will not be believers, though you are eager.

(104) وَما تَسْئَلُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ for the conveyance [of the message] مِنْ أَجْرٍ meaning, any recompense (جُعْل). إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ an admonition (عظة) from Allah لِلْعالَمِينَ universally. وَما تَسْئَلُهُمْ (١٠٤) عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِلْعالَمِينَ (104) You do not ask them any reward for it. It is just a reminder for all the nations.

(105) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ آيَةٍ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ which indicates Allah's wisdom (حكمة) and His power (قدرة) in His creation (صنع). يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْها and they observe it وَهُمْ عَنْها مُعْرِضُونَ They do not reflect upon it (يتفكّرون) nor do they take heed (يعتبرون) from it. وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ آيَةٍ (١٠٥) فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْها وَهُمْ عَنْها مُعْرِضُونَ (105) How many a sign there is in the heavens and the earth that they pass by, while they turn away from it!

(106) وَما يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللهِ إِلَّا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ ** (106) And most of them do not believe in Allah except while they are polytheists. [This polytheism is] in obedience (الطاعة) and by looking at the means (الأسباب).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It is] the shirk (شرك) of obedience (طاعة) and not the shirk of worship (عبادة).

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi added: And the acts of disobedience (المعاصي) which they commit are the shirk (شرك) of obedience (طاعة), in which they obeyed Satan, thus associating partners with Allah in obedience to other than Him. It is not the shirk (اشراك) of worship (عبادة) that they worship other than Allah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He obeys Satan from where he does not know, and thus commits shirk (يشرك).

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): They are those who deviate (يُلْحِدُونَ) concerning His Names (أسمائه) without knowledge, placing them in positions other than their rightful ones.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is the man who says, "Were it not for so-and-so, I would have perished," or "Were it not for so-and-so, I would have attained such-and-such," or "Were it not for so-and-so, my dependents would have been lost." Do you not see that he has made a partner for Allah in His dominion (ملكه), who provides for him and defends him? It was asked: "What if he says, 'Were it not that Allah favored me with so-and-so, I would have perished'?" He said: "Yes, there is no harm in this."

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Part of that is the man's saying, "No, by your life (وحياتك)." And from both of them (peace be upon them): The shirk (شرك) of blessings (النعم). And from al-Rida (peace be upon him): A shirk (شرك) by which one does not reach disbelief (الكفر).

footnote (١) The impotent man (*العنين*) is he who is unable to approach women and does not desire women, and an impotent woman (*امرأة عنّينة*) is she who does not desire men.

(١٠٧) أَفَأَمِنُوا أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ غاشِيَةٌ مِنْ عَذابِ اللهِ ** (107) Have they become secure that there should come upon them a pall of Allah’s punishment? A punishment that covers them and encompasses them, أَوْ تَأْتِيَهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً ** or that the Hour should overtake them suddenly, suddenly (فجأة), without any preceding sign (علامة), وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** while they are unaware? of its coming, being unprepared for it.

(١٠٨) قُلْ هذِهِ سَبِيلِي ** (108) Say, 'This is my way: It means the call to monotheism (التوحيد) and preparation for the Return (المعاد). أَدْعُوا إِلَى اللهِ ** I invite to Allah An explanation of the way (السبيل). عَلى بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِي. with insight—I and those who follow me.'

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That refers to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and the Successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them) after them both.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Ali followed him.

And narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him) when they objected to his young age, he said: "And what do they object to? Allah said to His Prophet: قُلْ هذِهِ سَبِيلِي (Say, 'This is my way')—the Ayah. By Allah, no one followed him except Ali, and he was nine years old, and I am nine years old."

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated that which is close to these narrations (الروايات). وَسُبْحانَ اللهِ ** and immaculate is Allah, And I declare Him immaculate with an absolute declaration of immaculacy (تنزيهاً). وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ.** and I am not one of the polytheists.'

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the interpretation of سُبْحانَ اللهِ ** (and immaculate is Allah). He said: "It is a declaration of Allah's transcendence (أنفة لله). Do you not see a man, when he is astonished by something, he says Subhan Allah?" And in another narration (رواية أخرى): He said: "It is a declaration of immaculacy (تنزيه)."

(١٠٩) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجالاً (And We did not send before you except men) — This is a refutation (ردّاً) of their saying: لَوْ شاءَ رَبُّنا لَأَنْزَلَ مَلائِكَةً (Had our Lord wished, He would have sent down angels) نُوحِي إِلَيْهِمْ (We reveal to them) — just as He revealed to you, and they were distinguished by that from others. And نُوحِي (We reveal) is also read with the nūn (بالنون). مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرى (from the people of the towns) — because its people are more knowledgeable (أعلم) and wiser (أحكم) than the people of the desert (أهل البدو).

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ (And We did not send before you) — meaning, to the creation (الى الخلق) — إِلَّا رِجالاً نُوحِي إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرى (except men to whom We revealed, from the people of the towns) — Thus, He informed that He did not send the angels to the earth to be leaders (أئمّة) or rulers (حكاماً); rather, they were sent to the Prophets of Allah.

أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ (Have they not traveled in the land) — Its interpretation has already preceded as the land of the Quran (بأرض القرآنِ). فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ (so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them?) — meaning, those who denied the Messengers and the signs (الآيات), so that they may beware of denying you, those who are passionately devoted (المشغوفين) to the world (الدنيا) and are desperate (المتهالكين) for it, so that they may detach themselves (فينقلعوا) from its love and practice asceticism (يزهدوا) regarding it. وَلَدارُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا (But the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are Godwary) — meaning, those who guarded against polytheism (الشرِّكَ) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). أَفَلا يَعْقِلُونَ (Do they not exercise their reason?) — meaning, they use their intellects (عقولهم) to know that it (the Hereafter) is better. And it is also read with the tāʾ (بالتاءِ) [referring to the verb يَعْقِلُونَ].

حَتَّى إِذَا اسْتَيْأَسَ الرُّسُلُ (until when the apostles despaired) — This is the culmination (غاية) of an omitted statement (كلام محذوف) indicated by the context. It is as if it was said: Our help to them was delayed, just as We delayed it for this nation, until when they despaired (استيأسوا) of victory. وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كُذِبُوا (and they thought that they had been lied to) — meaning, the Messengers thought that their people had lied to them (كذّبهم قومهم) regarding what they (the Messengers) had promised concerning the punishment and the victory over them. And كُذِبُوا is also read with the lightened dhāl (بالتخفيف) [i.e., كُذِبُوا instead of كُذِّبُوا]. In al-Jawami’ (في الجوامع), it is stated that this is the reading of the Imams of Guidance (أئمّة الهدى) (peace be upon them). Its meaning is: The people to whom the Messengers were sent (المرسل إليهم) thought that the Messengers had lied to them regarding what they informed them about Allah’s help to them.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كُذِبُوا (and they thought that they had been lied to) — read with the lightened dhāl (مخففة). He said: The Messengers thought that the devils (الشياطين) had appeared to them (تمثل لهم) in the form of angels (على صورة الملائِكةِ).

جاءَهُمْ نَصْرُنا (Our help came to them) — by sending down the punishment upon the disbelievers (الكفّار). فَنُجِّيَ مَنْ نَشاءُ (Then We delivered whom We wished) — meaning, We save (فنخلص) مَنْ نَشاءُ (whom We wished) — from the punishment when it descends, and they are the believers (المؤمنون). And فَنُجِّيَ is also read as a past tense verb built for the passive voice (على الماضي المبني للمفعوُل). وَلا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُنا عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ (and Our might is not averted from the guilty lot) — when it descends.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) concerning what al-Ma’mun asked Him about the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets: Allah says: حَتَّى إِذَا اسْتَيْأَسَ الرُّسُلُ (until when the apostles despaired) — meaning, of their people — وَظَنّ قومهم أنّ الرسل قَدْ كُذِبُوا (and their people thought that the apostles had been lied to) — جاءَهُمْ (Our help came to them). The Messengers' help (نَصْرُنا) came to them.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah entrusted them (وكلهم) to their own souls, so they thought that the devils (الشياطين) had appeared to them (قد تمثلت لهم) in the form of angels (في صورة الملائكة).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah entrusted them (وكلهم) to their own souls for less than the blink of an eye (أقلّ من طرفة عين). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked how the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not fear, regarding what came to him from Allah, that it might be something with which Satan (الشيطان) incites (ينزغ به). He replied: "Verily, when Allah chooses a servant as a Messenger, He sends down upon him tranquility (السكينة) and solemnity (الوقار), and what comes to him from Allah is like what He sees with His eye."

(١١١) لَقَدْ كانَ فِي قَصَصِهِمْ (111) There is certainly a lesson in their accounts the stories of the Prophets and their nations عِبْرَةٌ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبابِ for those who possess intellect. meaning, those who possess perfect intellects (العقول الكاملة). ما كانَ This Qur’ān is not the Qur'an حَدِيثاً يُفْتَرى a fabricated discourse, that is invented (يُخْتَلَق). وَلكِنْ تَصْدِيقَ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ but a confirmation of what was before it, before it, of the Divine Books (الكتب الإِلهيّة). Al-Qummi said: meaning, from the books of the Prophets. وَتَفْصِيلَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ and an elaboration of all things, that is needed in the religion (الدين) وَهُدىً and a guidance from error وَرَحْمَةً and a mercy by which one attains the good of both abodes (الدارين) لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ for a people who have faith. who believe in it.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Yusuf every day or every night, Allah will raise him on the Day of Resurrection, and his beauty will be upon the beauty of Yusuf (peace be upon him). Fear will not afflict him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will be among the best of Allah's righteous servants (عباد الله الصالحين).

And al-Ayyashi added: And he will be safe in this world (الدنيا) from being an adulterer (زانياً) or an obscene person (فحاّشاً). And in Thawab al-A'mal, he said: And it was written in the Torah (التوراة).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Do not teach your women Surah Yusuf (peace be upon him), nor let them recite it, for indeed, in it are temptations (الفتن). Rather, teach them Surah al-Nur, for indeed, in it are admonitions (المواعظ).

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is disliked (يُكْرَه) for them (women) to learn Surah Yusuf (peace be upon him).
